Theoretical Framing: "Tommy is an autonomous being who is entitled to the protections afforded by the New York law for legal persons and is entitled to petition this Court for his liberty" (Page 16 of petition).

Synopsis: In 2017, a New York court denied petitions by the Nonhuman Rights Project to declare two chimpanzees, Tommy and Kiko, legal entities with rights.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of Chucho the andean spectacled bear
Theoretical Framing: "Lo expresado implica modificar el concepto de sujeto de derecho en relación con la naturaleza, flexibilizando la perspectiva de que, quien es titular de derechos correlativamente está obligado a cumplir deberes; aceptando entonces ahora, que los sujetos sintientes no humanos, aun cuando son sujetos de derechos no poseen recíprocamente deberes. En esta órbita, por tanto, son sujetos de derechos sin deberes, o en cuanto que a estos no se les pueden imponer obligaciones por tratarse precisamente de sujetos de derecho sintientes, frente a quienes el principal guardador, representante, agente oficioso y responsable es el hombre en forma individual o colectiva. Si se considera que no pueden ser sujetos de derecho por no estar gravados con deberes recíprocamente, significa navegar en un auto-antropocentrismo individualista o colectivista, totalmente egoísta y reduccionista, para ver como iguales a quienes son totalmente diferentes, a pesar de constituir, parte esencial de la cadena biótica con peculiaridades propias." (Page 14, 15)
Initiating Actor Name: Luis Domingo Gomez Maldonado
Ecological Actor Name: Andean spectacled bear, tremarctos ornatus, named "Chucho".
Synopsis: The Colombian Constitutional Court overturned a lower court ruling that animals feel and are subjects of rights in 2020.

Title: II Inter-American Congress on the Environmental Rule of Law
Theoretical Framing: The two high-level panels reviewed different examples that were presented from throughout the Americas to illustrate the link between human rights and the environment, including the rights of the Atrato River case (pg 7) and a presented paper on the development and implementation of the rights of nature (pg 10).
Initiating Actor Name: OAS, IUCN, World Commission on Environmental Law, UN Environment, Poder Judicial, Global Judicial Institute on the Environment, and Facultad de Derecho
Synopsis: In September 2017, the Inter-American Congress on the Environmental Rule of Law met to discuss environmental rule of law trends from the Americas.

Title: Colorado (USA) District Court Case: Colorado River Ecosystem v. State of Colorado
Theoretical Framing: "...the Plaintiffs are asking this Court to recognize and declare that the Colorado River is capable of possessing rights similar to a "person," and that as part of that declaration, that the Colorado River has certain rights to exist, flourish, regenerate, and naturally evolve" (Page 3 of claim).
Initiating Actor Name: Deep Green Resistance; United States District of Colorado
Synopsis: The court dismissed a first-in-the-nation lawsuit in 2017 requesting the court recognize the rights of the Colorado River and grant the river “personhood”.

Title: Australia Yarra River Protection Act (2017)
Theoretical Framing: "to recognise that Crown land and freehold land owned by  the State,  that is  adjacent to the Yarra  River  and  which is used as public open space  or as a  park, is part of the  one living  and integrated natural entity,  and to protect that land..." (Page 10).
Initiating Actor Name: Australian Government and Wurundjeri people
Synopsis: In 2017, Australia passed the Yarra River Protection Act giving the Yarra River status as a legal entity and including Wurundjeri people in managing the river.

Title: Inter-American Court of Human Rights Advisory Opinion 23/17: environment and human rights (2017)
Theoretical Framing: "Esta Corte considera importante resaltar que el derecho al medio ambiente sano como derecho autónomo, a diferencia de otros derechos, protege los componentes del medio ambiente, tales como bosques, ríos, mares y otros, como intereses jurídicos en sí mismos, aún en ausencia de certeza o evidencia sobre el riesgo a las personas individuales. Se trata de proteger la naturaleza y el medio ambiente no solamente por su conexidad con una utilidad para el ser humano o por los efectos que su degradación podría causar en otros derechos de las personas, como la salud, la vida o la integridad personal, sino por su importancia para los demás organismos vivos con quienes se comparte el planeta, también merecedores de protección en sí mismos. En este sentido, la Corte advierte una tendencia a reconocer personería jurídica y, por ende, derechos a la naturaleza no solo en sentencias judiciales sino incluso en ordenamientos constitucionales". (Page 29).
Initiating Actor Name: Inter-American Court of Human Rights
Synopsis: The Inter-American Court of Human Rights’ 2017 advisory opinion mentioned rights of nature and connected human rights and environmental protection.

Title: New Zealand Law: Taranaki Maunga Collective Redress
Theoretical Framing: "...the declaration of a legal personality for Nga Maunga and the vesting of all available Crown-owned land in Nga Maungain that legal personality" (Page 10).
Initiating Actor Name: Maori; The Crown; Paul Goldsmith (Minister for Treaty of Waitangi Negotiations);Katherine Gordon (Chief Crown Negotiator);
Synopsis: In 2017, a record of understanding between iwi of Taranaki and the New Zealand government declared Mount Taranaki to have a legal personality.

Title: Plymouth (USA) Town Ordinance: community rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems within Plymouth possess the right to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve" (Page 4).
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and residents
Synopsis: In 2018, Plymouth, New Hampshire adopted a community bill of rights recognizing rights of nature and banning land acquisition for unsustainable energy projects.

Title: Bonito (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: Rights of Nature "The Municipality recognizes the right of nature to exist, thrive and evolve" (art 236)
Initiating Actor Name: MAPAS
Synopsis: On December 21, 2017, the municipality of Bonito, Brazil adopted an amendment recognizing the right of nature to exist, thrive, and evolve.

Title: Oregon (USA) Court Case: Justice v. Vercher
Theoretical Framing: "Justice is asking the Court to take these well-established rules to the logical next step and recognize that as a member of the class intended to be protected by Oregon’s anti-cruelty statute, Justice may bring a negligence per se claim based on the standard of care in the anti-cruelty statute" (Page 2).
Initiating Actor Name: Animal Legal Defense Fund; Kim Mosiman (Justice's guardian)
Synopsis: The court ruled dismissed a complaint against defendant Gwendolyn Vercher on behalf of the plaintiff, a neglected horse named Justice in 2018.

Title: Paudalho (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: Rights of Nature "The city recognizes the right of Nature to exist, prosper and evolve" Article 181.
Initiating Actor Name: Municipality of Paudalho; Mapas (NGO)
Synopsis: In 2018, the Municipality of Paudalho in the State of Pernambuco, Brazil adopted an amendment to the Organic Law recognizing the Rights of Nature.

Title: Uttarakhand (India) High Court Case: Bhatt v. Union of India
Theoretical Framing: "The entire animal kingdom including avian and aquatic are declared as legal entities having a distinct persona with corresponding rights, duties and liabilities of a living person" (Page 50).
Initiating Actor Name: Judge Rajiv Sharma and Judge Alok Singh (High Court of Uttarakhand)
Synopsis: In 2018, the High Court of Uttarakhand ruled that the entire animal kingdom including avian and aquatic are declared as legal entities.

Title: Crestone (USA) Town Resolution: Rights of Nature
Theoretical Framing: "The town of Crestone does officially recognize that nature, natural ecosystems, communities, and all species possess intrinsic and inalienable rights which must be effectuated to protect life on Earth" (Page 1).
Initiating Actor Name: Earth Law Center, and Mayor Danforth, and community
Ecological Actor Name: Nature, natural ecosystems, communities, and all species
Synopsis: On July 9 2018, the board of trustees in Crestone, Colorado adopted a resolution recognizing rights of nature.

Title: Ecuador constitutional court case: mining concessions in A’i Cofán of Sinangoe
Theoretical Framing: "These events represent a serious violation of rights and constitute a serious threat for the future, given the vast extension of the mining concessions; among them, the right to live in a healthy and ecologically balanced environment, established in article 14 of the Constitution, as well as the rights of Nature, as a subject of rights..." Synopsis: The court declared mining concessions violated rights of nature and the rights of the A'L Cofán Sinangoe people to water and a healthy environment.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of the Pisba Páramo
Theoretical Framing: "Tercero. DECLARAR que el Páramo de Pisba es sujeto de derechos, con los alcances señalados en la parte motiva de esta providencia." (Page 67).

Title: Ecuador Constitutional Court Case: rights of the Piatúa River

Initiating Actor Name: Lopez Huatotoca Nicolas Carlos, Charpentier Stacey Andres Felipe, Valenzuela Rosero Jose Feliciano, Aguinda Pilla Cristian Rigoberto, Granda Garrido Andre Mauricio, Villarroel Villegas Enid Susana, Sarzosa Santos Lenin Espartaco, Reyes Gomez Jacinto Rigoberto, Melo Cevallos Mario Efrain, Vargas Santi Marlon Richardm, Curipallo Alava Yajaira Anabel

Title: Santa Fe (Argentina) City Ordinance: aerial toxicities and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "...siendo la responsable de llevar a cabo las acciones de difusión, prevención, control y sanción de la presente ordenanza como así también de la promoción de medidas alternativas para el control de malezas y plagas, en armonía con el ambiente, la salud humana y los derechos de la naturaleza." Title: Mexico City (Federal District) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "El derecho a la preservación y protección de la naturaleza será garantizada por las autoridades de la Ciudad de México en el ámbito de su competencia, promoviendo siempre la participación ciudadana en la materia. 3. Title: White Earth Nation (USA) Tribal Resolution: Rights of Manoomin

Title: Ho-Chunk Nation (USA) Constitutional Amendment: Rights of Nature

Title: Ecuador court case: GMOs in Los Ríos Province

Title: Bangladesh court case: rights of the Turag River

Synopsis: The Bangladesh High Court ruled rivers in Bangladesh require protection and illegal activities must be stopped.

Title: Uganda National Environmental Act of 2019

Initiating Actor Name: Advocates for Natural Resources and Development (ANARDE), NAPE, AFRICE, and Gaia Foundation
Synopsis: In 2019, Uganda enacted the National Environmental Act of 2019, recognizing rights of nature and invoking ecological jurisprudence values.

Title: Toledo (USA) Charter Amendment: Lake Erie Bill of Rights
Theoretical Framing: "Lake  Erie,  and the  Lake  Erie  watershed, possess the right  to exist, flourish, and naturally  evolve" (Page 1)

Synopsis: The Lake Erie Bill of Rights recognized Lake Erie and the Lake Erie watershed, possess the right to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve in 2018.

Title: Nottingham (USA) Ordinance (2019): chemical trespass, community rights, and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities within Nottingham possess the right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate, evolve, and be restored, which shall include the right to be free from all corporate activities that threaten these rights, including chemical trespass resulting from the physical deposition or disturbance of toxic wastes." (Page 2)

Synopsis: Nottingham, New Hampshire's Freedom from Chemical Trespass Ordinance, which recognized rights of nature, was overturned in 2021.

Title: Colombia municipal court case: rights of the La Plata River

Synopsis: In 2019, the Civil Municipal Court of La Plata-Huila ruled in favor of the residents of El Remolino neighborhood and the rights of the La Plata river.

Title: Brazil Superior Court of Justice Case: guarding of wild animals
Theoretical Framing: Grants the provisional protection of a wild animal (parrot) based on the violation of the ecological dimension of the principle of human dignity. (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Superior Court of Justice of Brazil
Synopsis: In 2019, a court in Brazil granted the provisional protection of a parrot based on the violation of the ecological dimension of the principle of human dignity.

Title: Santa Fe Province (Argentina) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: “…creemos que una nueva Constitución Provincial debe reflejar esa diversidad y esa heterogeneidad de modos de relacionarnos con la naturaleza que no sólo permiten pensar este tema exclusivamente a partir del derecho a un ambiente sano sino que esto necesariamente debe articularse con los derechos de la naturaleza.” (Page 36)
Initiating Actor Name: M. Valeria Berros, Rafael Colombo y Natalia Barrilis
Synopsis: In 2017, an amendment to the Constitution of the Province of Santa Fe was proposed to better reflect human relationships to nature and the rights of nature.

Title: Belgium Court Case: Trees in Lennik v State of Belgium

Synopsis: In 2014, a court declared the Belgian government and local governments violate human rights and standards of care by having inadequate climate policies.

Title: Yurok Tribal Resolution: rights of the Klamath River 2019
Theoretical Framing: "That the Yurok Tribal Council now establishes the Rights of the Klamath River to exist, flourish and naturally evolve; to have a clean and healthy environment free from pollutants; to have a stable climate free from human-caused climate change impacts; and to be free from contamination by genetically engineered organisms." Initiating Actor Name: Yurok Tribal Council
Synopsis: In 2019, the Yurok Tribe made a resolution recognizing rights of the Klamath River and gave it legal personhood.

Title: Ahuachapán (El Salvador) Draft Ordinance: rights of La Laguna El Espino

Title: Colombia court case: rights of the Coello, Combeima, and Cocora Rivers
Theoretical Framing: "CUARTO: RECONOCER a los ríos Coello, Combeima y Cocora, sus cuencas y afluentes como entidades individuales, sujeto de derechos a la protección, conservación, mantenimiento y restauración a cargo del Estado y las comunidades." (Page 149)

Title: Punjab (India) High Court Case: Singh et al. vs State of Haryana
Theoretical Framing: "The entire animal kingdom including avian and aquatic are declared as legal entities having a distinct persona with corresponding rights, duties and liabilities of a living person. Synopsis: In 2004 in India, a judge declared “the entire animal kingdom” as legal entities with rights in a case related to the transportation of cows for meat slaughter.

Theoretical Framing: "Que los bosques son entidades vivientes, claves para el medio ambiente, por su captura de carbono, generación de oxigeno, recarga hídrica, conservación del suelo y biodiversidad biológica; por lo que cada ser humano debe tener el compromiso de cuidarlos, preservarlos, respetarlos y promover acciones concretar que amplien su cobertura en el territorio nacional, ya que sin naturaleza nosotros no existimos, somos parte de la naturaleza." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Legislative Assembly of El Salvador
Synopsis: In 2019, the Legislative Assembly of El Salvador called on the public opinion, public and private sectors to join efforts to address environmental issues.

Title: Ecuador court case: Mirador copper mine project

Theoretical Framing: "La naturaleza, conformada por todos sus ecosistemas y especies como un ente colectivo sujeto de derechos, deberá ser respetada en su existencia, en su restauración y en la regeneración de sus ciclos naturales, así como la conservación de su estructura y funciones ecológicas, en los términos que la ley lo establezca." Synopsis: In 2019, the superior court of Medellin ruled in favor of the rights of the Cauca River and of future generations.

Title: Nariño (Colombia) Regional Decree 348: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "ARTÍCULO PRIMERO: La Gobernación de Nariño promoverá el respeto, protección, conservación y restauración de los ecosistemas estratégicos del Departamento, las áreas de especial protección ecológica y de la naturaleza en su generalidad, adoptándolos como titulares de derechos y sujetos de protección." Title: Colombia court case: rights of the Otún River
Theoretical Framing: "RECONOCER al Río Otún, su cuenca y afluentes como una entidad sujeto de derechos a la protección, conservación, mantenimiento, y restauración del mismo a cargo de las entidades del orden municipal, departamental y nacional aquí enunciadas, de manera conjunta y dentro del ejercicio de sus competencias." Synopsis: In 2019, a ruling in Columbia recognized the Otún River, its basin and tributaries as an entity subject to rights.

Title: Pennsylvania (USA) State Constitutional Amendment: right of local self-government and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "...protecting health, safety and welfare by establishing the rights of people, their communities and nature and by securing those rights using prohibitions and other means..." (Page 1)

Title: Colombia court case: rights of the Magdalena River
Theoretical Framing: "TERCERO: RECONOCER al Rio Magdalena, su cuenca y afluentes como una entidad sujeto de derechos a la protección, conservación, mantenimiento y restauración a cargo del Estado, Enel-Emgesa y la comunidad, como se fundamentó en la parte motiva" (Page 35).

Theoretical Framing: " (...)
