(Page 63)
Initiating Actor Name: Rolando López Crisóstomo (municipality of Comitancillo); José Luis Ramírez (Indigenous Mayor of the municipality of San Juan); Victor Manuel Sales Ortiz (Maya Mam Saq Tx'otx Council); Angel Roblero Bravo (ADIMA); Juan Lucas Díaz (Mam Council); Ramón Clemente Ixcol Chávez (Indigenous Mayor's Office of the municipality of Santa Lucía Utatlán); Miguel Angel Manuel Alvarado (ancestral Maya Achí authorities of the municipality of Rabinal); Pedro Benjamín Barreno Zapeta and Santiago Domingo García Chamorro (department of Totonicapán); Seledonia Vicente Vicente (Maya K'iche' Women's Network of the municipality of Momostenango); personal capacity: Silvia Elizabeth Conoz Téllez, Irma Yolanda Tuc Quemé, Favián Enrique Castañeda Barrios, Narciso Nicolás Cua Culuz, Orla Marina Siquiná de Siquiná and Rigoberto Juarez Mateo
Synopsis: In 2019, the Constitutional Court of Guatemala ordered the development of a right to water and recognize the relationship between indigenous peoples and water.

Title: Colombia Court Case: Pueblo Awá and Katsa Su Territorial and Ecological Rights
Theoretical Framing: "ACREDITAR como víctimas en su calidad de sujetos colectivos de derecho, al Katsa Su gran territorio Awá y los 32 cabildos indígenas, asociados y representados por la UNIDAD INDÍGENA DEL PUEBLO AWÁ – ASOCIACIÓN DE AUTORIDADES TRADICIONALES INDÍGENAS AWÁ – UNIPA. Por tanto, reconocerle su calidad de interviniente especial como representante de los sujetos colectivos asociados a la UNIPA, de conformidad con lo señalado en la parte motiva de la decisión." (Page 38)
Initiating Actor Name: Unidad Indígena del Pueblo Awá – Asociación de Autoridades Tradicionales Indígenas Awá – UNIPA
Ecological Actor Name: Katsa Su - Awá territory and 32 indigenous councils (cabildos indígenas) represented by the UNIDAD INDÍGENA DEL PUEBLO AWÁ –ASOCIACIÓN DE AUTORIDADES TRADICIONALES INDÍGENAS AWÁ –UNIPA.
Synopsis: The Special Jurisdiction for Peace in Colombia recognized the Katsa Su - Awá territory and indigenous councils as victims as collective subjects of law in 2019.

Title: Buliisa (Uganda) District Resolution: Bagungu customary law and sacred sites (2019)
Theoretical Framing: "that sacred natural sites and territories are central to the cultural values, morals, and traditions and the customary laws of the Bagungu clans" (1)
Initiating Actor Name: Buliisa District Council; Indigenous Bagungu People; Association of Bagungu Custodians of Sacred Natural Sites
Ecological Actor Name: Sacred Natural Sites within Bagungu land
Synopsis: In 2019, the Buliisa District Council in Uganda recognized customary laws of Bagungu indigenous communities through a resolution.

Title: Philippines Rights of Nature Act
Theoretical Framing: "It is hereby declared the policy of the State to recognize and protect natural ecosystems and processes, or distinct and identifiable portions thereof as legal entities possessing certain fundamental and inalienable rights." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Senator Risa Hontiveros (2019); Rep. Kit Belmonte (2019); Rep. Joey Sarte Salceda (2023); Rights of Nature PH
Synopsis: In 2019, for the first time, national legislation recognizing the rights of nature as legal entities was introduced in the Philippines House and Senate.

Title: Western Australia Rights of Nature and Future Generations Act 2019
Theoretical Framing: "Nature, including all ecosystems, ecological communities and native species, has the right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate and evolve; recovery, rehabilitation and restoration; a healthy and stable climate system; and a vibrant and biodiverse community of life." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: MP Diane Evers (Western Australian Parliament)
Synopsis: In 2019, a bill was introduced in the Western Australian Parliament to recognize the rights of nature to exist, flourish, regenerate, and evolve.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of the Quindío River
Theoretical Framing: "PRIMERO: DECLARAR que el Rio Quindío, desde su nacimiento, su cuenca, afluentes y hasta su desembocadura, ostenta el trato de sujeto de derechos a la protección, conservación, mantenimiento y restauración a cargo del Estado, a través de las entidades accionadas y por los términos antes expuestos." (Page 65)
Initiating Actor Name: Judge Rigoberto Reyes Gomez
Synopsis: In 2019, due to contaminant minerals in the water of the City of Armenia, the administrative court of Quindio decided to grant the Quindio River rights.

Title: Mexico Federal Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Reconocer en el Artículo 1 de nuestra Carta Magna que debemos superar la visión antropocéntrica y proponernos establecer los derechos de la naturaleza al mismo nivel de los derechos humanos. También, en el Artículo 2 consideramos la importancia de hacer todo lo posible para hacer valer los derechos de los pueblos originarios, campesinos e indígenas en lo relacionado a la gestión de sus territorios bajo las formas de respeto a los derechos de la naturaleza, que es la forma que en su mayoría tienen estas comunidades en su la relación del día a día con la Madre Tierra." (Page 6)
Initiating Actor Name: Deputy Max Agustín Correa Hernández (Morena Party)
Synopsis: In 2019, a bill submitted by Deputy Max Correa sought to amend the Constitution of Mexico to establish the Rights of Nature.

Title: Menominee Tribe of Wisconsin (USA) Resolution: Rights of the Menominee River
Theoretical Framing: "the Tribe recognizes to protect the Menominee River, it must secure the highest protections for the river through the recognition of the river's inherent and legal rights" (Page 2).
Initiating Actor Name: Menominee Tribal Legislature
Synopsis: In 2020, the legislature of the Menominee Tribe of Wisconsin adopted a resolution recognizing the rights of the Menominee River.

Title: Frome (UK) Draft Bye-law: Rights for the River Frome
Theoretical Framing: "Rodden Meadow shall have the right to exist, thrive, regenerate, and evolve, and the right to restoration to high or good ecological status." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Councilor Peter Macfadyen; Friends of the River Frome
Ecological Actor Name: The Frome River and Rodden Meadow
Synopsis: In July 2016, a bylaw giving the Frome River and the Rodden Meadow rights and legal personhood was proposed to the town council of Frome, England.

Title: Romania Law: recognition of dolphins as nonhuman persons
Theoretical Framing: Dolphins in Romania enjoy the following rights: "the right to life"..."the right to bodily integrity and not to be subjected to acts of cruelty"... "the right to freedom of movement in the environment of life and not to be captured and held captive for any other purpose is to be treated medically or to be saved from imminent danger"..."the right to be protected in the living environment and not to be deprived of the group or family to which one belongs." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: MP Remus Cernea
Synopsis: In 2014, the Romanian upper house voted against a bill declaring dolphins as non-human persons with rights.

Title: New York (USA) court case: Nonhuman Rights Project v. Breheny
Theoretical Framing: "Autonomous  nonhuman  animals  such  as  Happy  should  have  'the  right  to  liberty protected  by  habeas  corpus'" (Page 2).
Initiating Actor Name: Nonhuman Rights Project
Synopsis: In 2022, an appellate judge ruled against a habeous corpus petition on behalf of Happy the elephant, and recognition of Happy's legal personhood and rights.

Title: ʔEsdilagh First Nation (Canada) Law: rights of the Sturgeon River
Theoretical Framing: "All Authorization holders must, at the holder's expense, take prompt action to remediate and restore the health of ʔElhdaqox for harm caused, directly or indirectly, under Authorization." (Page 9)
Initiating Actor Name: ʔEsdilagh First Nation
Synopsis: In 2020, the ʔEsdilagh First Nation, an indigenous group located in British Columbia, adopted a law in order to protect the Sturgeon River.

Title: Buckingham County U.S. draft ordinance James River Natural Community Bill of Rights
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities within Buckingham County possess the right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate, evolve, and be restored" (Page 2).
Initiating Actor Name: Buckingham: We the People (BWTP) (NGO); CELDF (NGO)
Synopsis: In 2018, the community group Buckingham proposed an ordinance recognizing rights of natural communities and ecosystems within Buckingham County, Virginia.

Title: Connecticut (USA) court case: NonHuman Rights Project v. Commerford Zoo
Theoretical Framing: "...Beulah, Minnie, and Karen are autonomous beings who live extraordinarily complex emotional, social, and intellectual lives and who possess those complex cognitive abilities sufficient for common law personhood and the common law right to bodily liberty protected by the common law of habeas corpus, as a matter of common law liberty, equality, or both." (Page 2 of petition)
Initiating Actor Name: NonHuman Rights Project
Synopsis: In 2017, the court denied NonHuman Rights Project’s petition to recognize the legal personhood of three elephants, Beulah, Karen and Minnie.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of Salamanca Island Park
Theoretical Framing: "En consecuencia, se declara a la zona protegida Vía Parque Isla de Salamanca como sujeto de derechos, en razón de lo consignado en las motivaciones." (Page 38) “Consequently, the protected area Salamanca Island Park is hereby declared to be subject to rights, based on the reasons stated in the grounds.”
Initiating Actor Name: Luis Miguel Llorente Altamiranda
Synopsis: The Colombian Supreme Court of Justice ultimately granted legal status to the Salamanca Island Parkway and ordered an emergency plan for its restoration.

Title: Nez Perce (USA) Tribal Resolution: Rights of the Snake River
Theoretical Framing: "The Nez Perce Tribe recognizes that the Snake River is a living entity that possesses fundamental rights, in accordance with long standing Nez Perce tribal beliefs and practices." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Nez Perce Tribe
Synopsis: In 2020, Nez Perce Tribe leaders passed a resolution recognizing the rights of Snake River and giving it legal personhood.

Title: Mexico New General Water Law Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Los habitantes del territorio nacional tienen el derecho de hacer uso de la Naturaleza para su bienestar dentro de los límites ecológicos sin contaminarla o explotarla, y reconocen que: i. Los seres humanos son parte de la Naturaleza, no están separados de ella, y deben esforzarse por vivir en armonía con los ríos, las fuentes de agua y toda la Naturaleza en general; ii. Los Derechos de la Naturaleza inlcuidos los Derechos de los Ríos y otras Fuentes Hídricas, y los Derechos Humanos al Agua, al Saneamiento y a un Medio Ambiente Sano son inseparables; iii. La Naturaleza es un ser vivo, indivisible, y está conformado por todos sus ecosistemas y especies; y iv. Por lo tanto se reconocen sus derechos como un ente colectivo sujeto de derechos." (Page 23)
Initiating Actor Name: Earth Law Center
Synopsis: In 2020, the Chamber of Deputies rejected amendments to the draft bill of the New General Water Law of Mexico adding the recognition of rights of nature.

Title: El Salvador Law: Rights of the Lempa River
Theoretical Framing: "1. Declara que el Río Lempa tiene derecho a los principios fundamentales expuestos en esta Declaración, que surgen de su propia existencia en nuestro planeta común; 2. Declara además que el Río Lempa es una entidad viviente que posee derecho de acceso a la justicia; 3. Establece que el Río Lempa deberá tener como mínimo los siguientes derechos fundamentales: (1) El derecho al flujo; (2) El derecho a ejercer sus funciones esenciales con el ecosistema; (3) El derecho a estar libre de toda contaminación; (4) El derecho a alimentar y ser alimentado por sus afluentes; (5) El derecho a la biodiversidad nativa; y, (6) El derecho a la regeneración y restauración." (Page 3) "1. Declares that the Lempa River has the right to the fundamental principles set forth in this Declaration, which arise from its very existence on our shared planet; 2. Further declares that the Lempa River is a living entity that has the right to access to justice; 3. Establishes that the Lempa River shall have at least the following fundamental rights: (1) The right to flow; (2) The right to perform its essential functions within the ecosystem; (3) The right to be free from all pollution; (4) The right to feed and be fed by its tributaries; (5) The right to native biodiversity; and, (6) The right to regeneration and restoration."
Synopsis: In 2020, a draft bill aimed to recognize the Lempa River as a subject of law in El Salvador, enabling a caretaking system.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of Los Nevados National Park
Theoretical Framing: "PRIMERO: DECLARAR que el Parque Nacional Natural los Nevados es Sujeto Especial de Derechos para su protección, recuperación y conservación con enfoque integral. Como sujeto declarado, se tutelan los derechos fundamentales del Parque Natural Nacional de los Nevados a la vida, a la salud y a un ambiente sano." (Page 106)
Initiating Actor Name: Juan Felipe Rodríguez Vargas
Synopsis: In 2020, the Superior Court of the Judicial District of Ibagué declared Los Nevados National Park as a special subject of rights.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of the Complejo del Páramo de Las Hermosas
Theoretical Framing: "PRIMERO: DECLARAR que el Complejo de Páramos Las Hermosas es Sujeto Especial de Derechos para su protección, conservación y preservación con enfoque integral. En consecuencia, se tutelan los derechos fundamentales del Complejo de Páramos Las Hermosas a la vida, a la salud y a un ambiente sano, debido a la falta de articulación y coordinación en las acciones adoptadas en beneficio del parque por las entidades nacionales, territoriales y las Corporaciones Autónomas accionadas en cumplimiento de sus deberes de cuidado, mantenimiento y conservación del parque." (Page 81)
Initiating Actor Name: Juan Felipe Rodríguez Vargas, Daniel Rubio Jiménez
Synopsis: In 2020, the Superior Court of the Judicial District of Ibagué in Colombia declared Complejo de Páramos "Las Hermosas" as a special subject of rights.

Title: El Salvador Proposed Constitutional Amendment to Recognize the Rights of Nature
Theoretical Framing: "Nature is also recognized as a subject of rights, since it generates, reproduces and realizes life. Recognized rights include the right to full respect for Nature's existence, the maintenance and regeneration of its vital cycles, structure, functions and evolutionary processes." (page 6)
Initiating Actor Name: Movimiento Político Rebelión Verde El Salvador (ReverdES)
Synopsis: A proposal for constitutional reform was presented to the Legislative Assembly of El Salvador to recognize rights of nature at the constitutional level in 2021.

Title: EU Parliament Resolution: halt and reverse EU-driven global deforestation
Theoretical Framing: "...while forests and biodiversity also have an intrinsic value beyond their use value to humans, including as carbon stocks, which cannot be monetised or quantified; I. whereas forests have cultural, social and spiritual value for many people and peoples;"(Page 6)...and..."Believes that ancient and primary forests should be considered and protected as global commons, and that their ecosystems should be granted a legal status." (Page 19)
Initiating Actor Name: European Parliament
Synopsis: In 2020, the EU Parliament passed a resolution addressing deforestation and the protection of forests in order to restore their biodiversity.

Title: Orange County (USA) Ordinance: rights of the Wekiva and Econlockhatchee Rivers
Theoretical Framing: "The Wekiva River and Econlockhatchee River, portions of which are within the boundaries of Orange County, and all other Waters within the boundaries of Orange County, have a right to exist. Flow, to be protected against Pollution and to maintain a healthy ecosystem." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF; Speak Up Wekiva; Eugene Stoccardo (member of the charter review commission)
Synopsis: In 2020, Orange County, Florida adopted a charter amendment recognizing the Wekiva River and Econlockhatchee River have rights.

Title: Argentina National Rights of Nature Law
Theoretical Framing: "La Naturaleza, o Pachamama, donde se reproduce y realiza la vida, tiene derecho a que se respete integralmente su existencia y el mantenimiento y regeneración de sus ciclos y funciones vitales, estructura y procesos evolutivos" (page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Leonardo Grosso, member of the Argentine Chamber of Deputies for Buenos Aires Province
Synopsis: In 2020, the Rights of Nature Law was reintroduced to the House of Representatives with the support of the Global Alliance for Rights of Nature.

Title: Oaxaca State (Mexico) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Los Derechos de la Naturaleza son: la preservación, la protección de los elementos que la conforman, a ejercer sus ciclos naturales y vitales y sus funciones ecológicas, a la restauración integral del equilibrio ecológico y a ser legalmente representadas". Estos derechos integral deberán de su ser equilibrio garantizados ecológico por y las aautoridades del Estado de Oaxaca, en el ámbito de sus competencias" (Page 9)
Initiating Actor Name: Deputies Fredie Delfin Avendaño and Horacio Soza Villavicencio in partnership with Claudia Brindis and Grant Wilson from Earth Law Center Mexico
Synopsis: In April 2021, the State of Oaxaca passed a state constitutional amendment recognizing the Rights of Nature.

Title: Santa Fe Province (Argentina) Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "La Naturaleza, donde se reproduce y realiza la vida, tiene derecho a que se respete integralmente su existencia y el mantenimiento y regeneración de sus ciclos y funciones vitales, estructura y procesos evolutivos." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Carlos del Frade, Provincial Deputy
Synopsis: In 2020, Representative Carlos del Frade submitted a Rights of Nature bill for the Santa Fe province in Argentina to achieve harmony with nature.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of Lago de Tota
Theoretical Framing: "SEGUNDO. – DECLARAR que el Lago de Tota y su cuenca hidrográfica, ostenta la calidad de sujeto de derechos a la protección, conservación, mantenimiento y restauración a cargo del Estado, en cabeza del Ministerio del Medio Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible, de conformidad con los argumentos expuestos en este proveído." (Page 71)
Initiating Actor Name: Judge Adriana Fernanda Guasguita Galindo
Synopsis: In 2020, the First Civil Court of Sogamoso, Boyacá, Colombia declared Lake Tota and its watershed as rights holders.

Title: IUCN World Conservation Congress Motion 218: recognizing nature as a subject of law
Theoretical Framing: "a) d’évaluer l’impact des droits de la Nature sur la biodiversité et les conditions de vie des populations, spécialement celles qui en sont les plus dépendantes ; b) ... à l’évolution du droit international et des droits internes vers un modèle moins anthropocentrique en promouvant l’octroi d’une personnalité juridique à la Nature en général et à certains de ses éléments en particulier ... c) d’appuyer et de favoriser la création et le développement de formations pertinentes sur les droits de la Nature" (Pages 1-2).
Initiating Actor Name: Earth Law Center
Synopsis: Lawyers from the IUCN submitted a motion to formally recognize Rights of Nature at the 2020 IUCN World Conservation Congress.

Title: Ecuador court case: construction of the Evenezer-Macuma-Taisha highway
Theoretical Framing: "(...) teniendo en consideración que los informes de la Autoridad Ambiental se refieren únicamente al ambiente sano y su afectación, sin embargo aplicando el principio de precaución cabría hacer referencia a que es probable que los derechos de la naturaleza establecidos en l4 Constitución pudieron haberse visto afectados y que finalmente es necesaria la restauración de área afectada, eso no deja de lado que existen otras garantías que el Estado debe exigir respecta a los responsables como son las medidas de no repetición; puesto que el fin último es de conservar estos sitios que tienen particular importancia para el Estado y para el respeto de lo; ciclos vitales, estructura y funciones de la naturaleza entendida como sujeto de derechos, es necesario que se mantenga la salvaguardia necesaria de toda actividad que provoque el detrimento del ecosistema." (Page 15 of Defensoria del Pueblo Report)
Initiating Actor Name: Ecuador's Ministry of Environment
Ecological Actor Name: Macuma-Macuma and Taisha rivers, Katuku-Shaimi Forest
Synopsis: In 2011, an Ecuador court halted the Provincial Government of Morona Santiago from constructing a highway, declaring it violated rights of nature.

Title: Ecuador Organic Code of the Environment
Theoretical Framing: "Article 1.- Purpose. The purpose of this Code is to guarantee the right of people to live in a healthy and ecologically balanced environment, as well as to protect the rights of nature for the realization of good living or sumak kawsay." (Page 8)
Initiating Actor Name: Ecuadorian National Assembly
Synopsis: The Organic Code on the Environment of Ecuador was published in 2017, guaranteeing environmental rights and rights of nature as recognized in the constitution.

Title: Ecuador court case: Llurimagua copper mine in the Intag Cloud Forest
Theoretical Framing: "the constitutional rights of nature have been violated, protected in the Constitution of the Republic, in Arts. 71 and 73." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Andrea Terán Valdez (plaintiff); DECOIN, GARN, CEDENMA
Ecological Actor Name: Ecosystems in the concession area in Llurimagua
Synopsis: In 2020, a judge in Ecuador ordered the suspension of activities related to the Llurimagua mining concession due to imminent violations of rights of nature.

Title: Florianópolis (Brazil) Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: Rights of Nature
Initiating Actor Name: Municipality of Florianopolis, Mapas (NGO)
Synopsis: In 2019, the city of Florianopolis in Brazil approved an amendment stating it is responsible for promoting diversity and harmony with nature.

Title: Paudalho (Brazil) Municipal Ordinance: San Severino Ramos Natural Water Spring
Theoretical Framing: Local law declares the San Severino Ramos Natural Water Spring as a natural, environmental and cultural heritage - necessary to ensure the right to an ecologically healthy and balanced environment with the maintenance of ecosystem processes necessary for the quality of life
Initiating Actor Name: Mayor of Paudalho
Ecological Actor Name: San Severino Ramos Natural Water Spring
Synopsis: In 2018, Paudalho, Brazil passed an ordinance recognizing the Fonte de Agua Mineral as part of a natural, environmental and cultural heritage.

Title: Universal Declaration of the Rights of Mother Earth
Theoretical Framing: "Mother Earth and all beings of which she is composed have the following inherent rights..." and "Every human being is responsible for respecting and living in harmony with Mother Earth." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: President Evo Morales Ayma of Bolivia
Synopsis: In 2010, the Universal Declaration of Rights of Mother Earth was presented at the First Peoples Conference on Climate Change and the Rights of Mother Earth.

Title: The Earth Charter
Theoretical Framing: "Recognize that all beings are interdependent and every form of life has value regardless of its worth to human beings." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Earth Charter Commission
Synopsis: In 2000, the Earth Charter Commission launched the Earth Charter at the Peace Palace in The Hague.

Title: Pakistan Supreme Court Case: D. G. Khan Cement Company v Government of Punjab
Theoretical Framing: "Man and his environment each need to compromise for the better of both and this peaceful co-existence requires that the law treats environmental objects as holders of legal rights." (Page 13)
Initiating Actor Name: Supreme Court of Pakistan (Justice Manzoor Ahmad Malik; Justice Syed Mansoor Ali Shah)
Synopsis: In 2021, the Supreme Court of Pakistan banned the construction of cement plants in environmentally vulnerable areas.

Title: Florianópolis (Brazil) Court Case: rights of the Conceição Lagoon
Theoretical Framing: "Regarding the recognition of Nature's rights, article 144 of the Law The Municipality's body granted nature the ownership of a right and in its sole paragraph determined that the Public Power will promote public policies and instruments for environmental monitoring so that nature acquires ownership of the right." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Uniao Florianopolitana Das Entidades Comunitarias (UFECO); Costa Legal; Associacao Pachamama
Synopsis: In 2021, a court in Brazil approved a lawsuit to protect the right to an ecologically-balanced environment over damage to the saltwater Conceição Lagoon.

Title: Hawaii (USA) Law: personhood of sacred species and natural ecosystems
Theoretical Framing: "'Person' includes individuals, partnerships, corporations, limited liability companies, associations, and sacred species and natural ecosystems. A sacred species or natural ecosystem may participate in the cooperative, through a guardian speaking on behalf, and in the best interest, of the sacred species or natural ecosystem." (Pages 6-7)
Initiating Actor Name: Elizabeth Dunne (ELC) and The Kohala Center
Synopsis: In 2021, a bill introduced in the Hawaii senate included a sacred species or a natural ecosystem in its definition of person.

Title: French Citizen Convention Ecological Transformation Bill
Theoretical Framing: Ecological transformation through ecocide
Initiating Actor Name: President of the Republic, Citizens Climate Convention
Synopsis: The French draft bill included a number of ecocide-like provisions that criminalize environmental damages in 2021.

Title: Vietnam 1999 Criminal Code Mentioning Ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Those who, in peace time or war time, commit acts of annihilating en-mass population in an area, destroying the source of their livelihood, undermining the cultural and spiritual life of a country, upsetting the foundation of a society with a view to undermining such society, as well as other acts of genocide or acts of ecocide or destroying the natural environment, shall be sentenced to between ten years and twenty years of imprisonment, life imprisonment or capital punishment." (Page 196)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Synopsis: In 2015, Vietnam overturned its 2000 criminal code and the inclusion of an article that criminalizes acts of ecocide.

Title: Russian Criminal Code (1996): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Massive destruction of the animal or plant kingdoms, contamination of the atmosphere or water resources, and also commission of other actions capable of causing an ecological catastrophe, Shall be punishable by deprivation of liberty for a term of 12 to 20 years." (Page 169)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Ecological Actor Name: "animal or plant kingdoms"..."atmosphere or water resources"..."ecological"
Synopsis: Russia added an article about ecocide to its criminal code in 1996.

Title: Kazakhstan Penal Code (1997): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Mass destruction of plant or animal world, poisoning the atmosphere, land or water resources, and also the commission of other offences, caused or able to cause ecological disaster are punished with the deprivation of liberty for a period of 10 to 15 years." (Page 38)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Ecological Actor Name: "plant or animal world"..."atmosphere"..."land or water resources"..."ecological"
Synopsis: Kazakhstan added an article about ecocide to its criminal code in 1997.

Title: Belarus Criminal Code (1999): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Mass destruction of flora or fauna, poisoning the atmosphere, land or water resources, as well as the commission of other acts which caused or a capable of causation of an ecological catastrophe, shall be punished by imprisonment for a period from ten to fifteen years." (Page 71)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Ecological Actor Name: "flora or fauna" ..."atmosphere, land or water resources"..."ecological"
Synopsis: Belarus amended it's 1997 criminal code to add an article about ecocide.

Title: Georgia Criminal Code (1999): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Ecocide i.e. contamination of the atmosphere, soil, water resources, mass destruction of fauna or flora, or any other act that could have led to an ecological disaster, shall be punished by imprisonment for a term of twelve to twenty years." (Page 151)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Synopsis: Georgia added an article about ecocide to its criminal code in 1999.

Title: Ukraine Criminal Code (2001): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Mass destruction of flora and fauna, poisoning of air or water resources, and also any other actions that may cause an environmental disaster, shall be punishable by imprisonment for a term of eight to fifteen years." (Page 90)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Synopsis: Ukraine added an article about ecocide to its criminal code in 2001.

Title: Republic of Moldova Penal Code (2002): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Deliberate mass destruction of flora and fauna, poisoning the atmosphere or water resources, and the commission of other acts that may cause or caused an ecological disaster shall be punished by imprisonment for 10 to 15 years." (Page 59)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Synopsis: Moldova added an article about ecocide to its criminal code in 2002.

Title: Armenia Criminal Code (2003): ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Mass destruction of flora or fauna, poisoning the environment, the soils or water resources, as well as implementation of other actions causing an ecological catastrophe, is punished with imprisonment for the term of 10 to 15 years." (Page 97)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Synopsis: Armenia added an article about ecocide to its criminal code in 2003.

Title: Ecuador Court Case on Hydrocarbon Particles Sprayed by the Company Petroamazonas EP
Theoretical Framing: "With the selection of case No. 27-21-JC also for its novelty, the Constitutional Court may specify the jurisprudential precedents regarding active legitimation, and particularly, it may develop, through binding jurisprudence, the parameters regarding the revocation of precautionary measures when the rights of nature and a healthy environment are involved." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Petroamazonas EP
Synopsis: The Constitutional Court of Ecuador selected a case involving restoration measures by the company Petroamazonas EP for the development of jurisprudence.

Title: Florida (USA) State Constitutional Amendment: right to clean water
Theoretical Framing: "The Everglades, Florida Springs, the Indian River Lagoon, the St. Johns River, the Caloosahatchee River, the Suwannee River, the Santa Fe River, Apalachicola Bay, Biscayne Bay, Tampa Bay, Pensacola Bay and all other Florida waters have a right to clean water, and that right shall include the rights of those waters to exist, flow, be free from pollution, and maintain a healthy ecosystem." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Florida Rights of Nature Network
Synopsis: In Florida, a petition is underway to get a constitutional amendment recognizing the right to clean water of all waterways in the state, on the general ballot.

Title: Florida (USA) State Constitutional Amendment: iconic species protection
Theoretical Framing: "Florida Iconic Species possess the legal right to exist, thrive, and evolve." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Florida Rights of Nature Network
Synopsis: In 2021, the FL5.org drafted a constitutional amendment for the state of Florida recognizing that Florida Iconic Species possess legal rights.

Title: Minganie (Canada) Municipal Resolution: rights of the Magpie River
Theoretical Framing: "Whereas the Minganie MRC recognizes that, in order to protect the Magpie River, its ecosystem, its species, and its residents, it must ensure the protection of the Magpie River by granting it legal personhood and rights;"
Initiating Actor Name: Minganie Regional County Municipality; Muteshekau-shipu Alliance
Synopsis: In February 2021, the Muteshekau-shipu Alliance announced the adoption of two parallel resolutions recognizing the Magpie River as a legal person with rights.

Title: Derry City and Strabane (Northern Ireland) Motion: exploring a declaration for the rights of nature
Location: Derry and Strabane District, Northern Ireland
Theoretical Framing: "Council asks for a report to come to Council on embedding ‘Rights of Nature’ as a keystone concept into Council’s operational practices, planning processes and long term decision making." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: People Before Profit councillor Maeve O'Neill
Synopsis: In 2021, a councilor from the Derry and Strabane District Council in Ireland made a motion for Council to hold two community workshops about rights of nature.

Title: Prince Charles of Wales Terra Carta Charter: For Nature People and Planet
Theoretical Framing: "As we strive to imagine the next 800 years of human progress, the fundamental rights and value of Nature must represent a step-change in our 'future of industry' and 'future of economy' approach." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Prince Charles of Wales; Sustainable Markets Initiative
Synopsis: In 2021, Prince Charles of Wales produced the Terra Carta, which provides a roadmap for businesses to work towards a sustainable future by 2030.

Title: Sweden Constitutional Amendment: Rights of Nature
Theoretical Framing: "Article 26. Nature, including ecosystems and native species, shall be guaranteed the following rights: 1. the right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate and develop; 2. the right to restoration, recovery and preservation; and 3. the right to perform their natural functions." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Rebecka Le Moine (MP); Pella Thiel (Rights of Nature Sweden)
Synopsis: In 2019, MP Rebecka Le Moine of the Swedish parliament proposed an amendment recognizing rights of nature to the Swedish constitution.

Title: Pakistan Court Case: Islamabad Wildlife Management Board v. Metropolitan Corporation Islamabad
Theoretical Framing: "Do the animals have legal rights? The answer to this question, without any hesitation, is in the affirmative." (Page 58)
Initiating Actor Name: Chief Justice Athar Minallah
Synopsis: In 2020, a justice in the Islamabad High Court in Pakistan ruled animals have legal rights in a case regarding the cruel treatment of zoo animals.

Title: Peru National Rights of Nature Law
Theoretical Framing: "Artículo 1.- Objeto de la Ley: La presente Ley tiene por objeto reconocer que la Madre Naturaleza, los ecosistemas y las especies son titulares de derechos y sujetos de protección por parte del Estado; por tratarse de entes vivos, con valor intrínseco y universal, que tienen derecho a existir, desarrollarse naturalmente, regenerarse, restaurarse y evolucionar. Cualquier persona, natural o jurídica, comunidades o pueblos indígenas, podrán exigir al Estado, cualquiera sea su nivel de gobierno, el cumplimiento de las disposiciones de la presente Ley." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: MP Lenin Fernando Bazan Villanueva
Synopsis: In 2021, a bill by Peruvian legislator Lenin Fernando Bazán Villanueva sought to recognize the rights of Mother Nature for the first time in Peru.

Title: Universal Declaration of the Rights of Rivers
Theoretical Framing: "Declares that all rivers are entitled to the fundamental rights set forth in this Declaration, which arise from their very existence on our shared planet...Further declares that all rivers are living entities that possess legal standing in a court of law." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Earth Law Center
Synopsis: In 2017, the Earth Law Center created a Universal Declaration of River Rights recognizing that rivers are living entities and entitled to fundamental rights.

Title: Oslo Manifesto for Ecological Law and Governance
Theoretical Framing: "This makes it possible to identify ecological approaches to law with a view to create a unifying framework for promoting effective law and governance. As we face disintegrating ecological and socio-economic systems and in light of greater, but still incomplete understanding of how ecosystems function and maintain their resilience, now is the time for creating an alternative." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: IUCN WCEL Ethics Specialist Group Workshop
Synopsis: In 2016, an interdisciplinary group of experts at an IUCN WCEL Ethics Specialist Group Workshop wrote the Olso Manifesto for Ecological Law and Governance.

Title: France Universal Declaration of Tree Rights
Theoretical Framing: “A tree is a living organism whose average lifespan is far longer than that of a human being. It should be respected throughout its life and have the right to develop and to reproduce freely, from its birth to its natural death, whether it be a town tree or a country tree. A tree should be considered as a subject of law, including when laws regarding human property are involved." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Déclaration Universelle des Droits de L'arbre; French National Assembly; MP Delphine Batho (president of Génération écologie party)
Synopsis: The French National Assembly adopted a universal declaration of tree rights in 2019 after a petition by the group Déclaration Universelle des Droits de L'arbre.

Title: The Hague Principles for a Universal Declaration on Responsibilities for Human Rights and Earth Trusteeship
Theoretical Framing: "Responsibilities for Nature, the Earth community and rights of Nature are grounded in the intrinsic values of nature and of all living beings." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Earth Trusteeship
Synopsis: In 2018, the organization Earth Trusteeship created the Hague Principles for a Universal Declaration on Responsibilities for Human Rights and Earth Trusteeship.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 1st International Hearing: Quito 2014
Theoretical Framing: "The Tribunal recognizes that in addition to the violation of the rights of Nature in the Gulf of Mexico, BP has generated serious negative ecological impacts elsewhere that may constitute additional violations of the rights of Nature." (Last paragraph of BP oil spill case)
Initiating Actor Name: International Tribunal for the Rights of Nature; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN)
Synopsis: In 2014, GARN held an international rights of nature tribunal in Quito, Ecuador to serve as a model for how courts could operate under rights of nature laws.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 2nd Regional Hearing: Europe 2021
Theoretical Framing: "We ask that the Swedish parliament recognize the rights of Lake Vättern according to the Declaration of the Rights of Lake Vättern." (Verdict from the Vättern case)
Initiating Actor Name: International Rights of Nature Tribunal; Global alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN)
Synopsis: In 2021, GARN held the European Rights of Nature Tribunal "In Defense of Aquatic Ecosystems" to investigate five critical aquatic ecosystem cases.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 1st Regional Hearing: Chile 2019
Theoretical Framing: "From the aforementioned rights, it can be concluded that any human activity that affects any of the rights recognized in favor of Mother Earth produces an ecological imbalance, seriously damaging its biocapacity, as well as the regeneration of cycles and vital processes of Mother Earth..." (Verdict of lithium mining case)
Initiating Actor Name: International Rights of Nature Tribunal; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN)
Synopsis: In 2019, GARN held a Regional Rights of Nature tribunal in Santiago, Chile where judges heard five cases on Rights of Nature violations in Latin America.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 4th International Hearing: Bonn 2017
Theoretical Framing: "The Tribunal found that in each of the seven cases, serious and systematic violations of the Universal Declaration of the Rights of Mother Earth (UDRME) had occurred, often accompanied by human rights violations, and in several cases the harm was so severe as to constitute ecocide." (Website introduction to tribunal)
Initiating Actor Name: International Tribunal for the Rights of Nature; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN)
Synopsis: In 2017, GARN held an international rights of nature tribunal in Bonn, Germany to serve as a model for how courts could operate under rights of nature laws.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 2nd International Hearing: Lima 2014
Theoretical Framing: "The Tribunal affirms that all persons, groups, and individual peoples have not only the right but the responsibility for protecting the rights of nature and condemns all these intimidating practices by companies and by the States, and stands in solidarity with all those affected." (Page 21)
Initiating Actor Name: International Tribunal for the Rights of Nature; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN)
Synopsis: In 2014, GARN held an international rights of nature tribunal in Lima, Peru to serve as a model for how courts could operate under rights of nature laws.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 3rd International Hearing: Paris 2015
Theoretical Framing: "After analyzing the evidence presented the Tribunal 1) condemns Texaco (now Chevron) for using technology known as inadequate, causing irreversible damage to the Amazon rainforest and violating of the rights of Nature and 2) orders the company to comply with the provisions of the Ecuadorian Tribunals and proceed to full compensation of the subject sites." (Chevron/Texaco case ruling)
Initiating Actor Name: International Tribunal for the Rights of Nature; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN)
Synopsis: In 2015, GARN held an international rights of nature tribunal in Paris, France where judges applied the Universal Declaration of the Rights of Mother Earth.

Title: Florida (USA) Court Case: Waters of Orange County v. Beachline South Residential
Theoretical Framing: "The Meridian Parks Remainder Project Application violates the rights of Orange County Waters to be protected against Pollution by filling 18.84 acres of wetlands to build stormwater ponds." (Page 10)
Initiating Actor Name: Orange County Waters; Charles O'Neil; Center for Democratic and Environmental Rights
Ecological Actor Name: Wilde Cypress Branch, Boggy Branch, Crosby Island Marsh, Lake Hart, Lake Mary Jane and all other Affected Orange County Waters
Synopsis: In 2021 bodies of water in Orange County, Florida sued a developer for violating their legal rights as established by the Florida Constitution in 2020.

Title: Citizen Declaration of the Rights of the Moon
Theoretical Framing: "The Moon possesses fundamental rights, which arise from its existence in the universe." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Thomas Gooch (Moon Village Association); Dr Michelle Maloney (Australian Earth Law Alliance), Ceridwen Dovey; Alice Gorman; Mari Margil (Center for Democratic and Environmental Rights, US)
Synopsis: In 2020, Thomas Gooch of the Moon Village Association spearheaded the Declaration of the Rights of the Moon, which aims to establish legal rights for the Moon.

Title: Uzbekistan 1994 Criminal Code Mentioning Ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Pollution or damage of land, water, or atmospheric air, resulted in mass disease incidence of people, death of animals, birds, or fish, or other grave consequences shall be punished..." (Page 38)
Initiating Actor Name: National Legislature
Synopsis: In 2003, Uzbekistan replaced it's 1994 criminal code, which eliminated article 196 and any mention of ecocide in its criminal code.

Title: European Citizens Initiative (ECI): draft directive on rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Nature shall have a legal personality, be recognized as having fundamental rights that arise from its inherent dignity as the source of life and those rights shall be respected, applied and protected by the law of the Union and the laws of the member states." (Page 12)
Initiating Actor Name: Nature's Rights (Mumta Ito)
Synopsis: In 2017, Mumta Ito, founder of Nature's Rights, presented a Draft Directive for Rights of Nature to members of the European parliament.

Title: Asheville (USA) City Ordinance: climate bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities within the City of Asheville possess the right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate, evolve, and be restored." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Community Roots
Synopsis: The NGO called Community Roots drafted a Climate Bill of Rights, which includes a rights of nature clause, for Asheville, North Carolina in 2020.

Title: Spain National Mar Menor Act of 2022
Theoretical Framing: "The Mar Menor and its basin are recognized as having the rights to protection, conservation, maintenance and, where appropriate, restoration, by the governments and the coastal inhabitants. It is also recognized the right to exist as an ecosystem and to evolve naturally..." (Art 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Professor Teresa Vicente Giménez
Synopsis: In 2022, Spain passed a national law establishing legal personhood for Mar Menor Lagoon, setting a European precedent for rights-based ecosystem protection.

Title: Argentina Supreme Court Case: Civil Association for Environmental Justice v. Province of Entre Ríos et al.
Theoretical Framing: "Declare Subject of Right to the 'Paraná Delta,' an essential ecosystem for mitigation and adaptation to climate change." (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: Asociación Civil por la Justicia Ambiental, Foto Ecologista de Paraná, and some children from the city of Rosario
Synopsis: In 2020, two NGOs asked the Argentine Supreme Court to declare Paraná Delta a subject with rights.

Title: Punjab (India) High Court Case: legal personhood for Sukhna Lake
Theoretical Framing: "We, by invoking our parens patriae jurisdiction,declare Sukhna Lake as legal entity/legal person/juristic person/ juridical person/moral person/artificial person for its survival, preservation and conservation having distinct persona with corresponding rights, duties and liabilities of a living person." (Page 143)
Initiating Actor Name: Sh. Gautam Khanna (resident)
Synopsis: In 2020, the High Court of Punjab India declared that Sukhna Lake has a "distinct persona with corresponding rights, duties, and liabilities of a living person"

Title: Nederland (USA) Town Resolution: Rights of Boulder Creek
Theoretical Framing: "The Creek and its encompassing Watershed, and the living and other things existing naturally therein, exist and function as an integrated and interdependent system of natural communities and are therefore understood, respected, and recognized in this Resolution as a living entity, possessing fundamental and inalienable rights." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Save the Colorado
Ecological Actor Name: Boulder Creek and Boulder Creek Watershed
Synopsis: In 2024, the town of Nederland, Colorado repealed a 2021 resolution recognizing the rights of Boulder Creek and the Boulder Creek Watershed.

Title: Williams County (USA) Charter Amendment: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "All people of the County of Williams, along with ecosystems in the County (including, but not limited to, rivers, streams, wetlands, and aquifers), possess the right to clean air, water, and soil; to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve; and to be free from activities that would violate these rights." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Williams County Alliance, CELDF
Synopsis: In 2019, the Williams County Alliance submitted a proposed County Charter for Williams County, Ohio recognizing rights of nature.

Title: Youngstown (USA) Town Charter: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "All people of the city of Youngstown, along with ecosystems and natural communities within the  city, possess the right to clean water, air, and soil, and to be free from  activities that would violate this right." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Protect Youngstown
Synopsis: In 2013, the civil action group Protect Youngstown submitted the first of nine charter amendments recognizing rights of nature.

Title: San Miguel County (USA) Ordinance: restricting oil and gas drilling
Theoretical Framing: "The oil and gas ordinance regulations are designed to...preserve and protect environmentally sensitive lands, including but not limited to: wildlife habitats, wetlands, hillsides, arroyos, acequias, floodplains, and cultural, historical, and archaeological resources." (Page 44)
Initiating Actor Name: Robert Freilich (a planning and zoning attorney), PROTECT San Miguel, San Miguel Oil and Gas Ordinance Task Force
Synopsis: In 2014, San Miguel County in New Mexico adopted a measure severely restricting oil and gas drilling.

Title: Bay Area (USA) People's Tribunal: fossil fuels and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "I find that the legal and economic system that we're caught in and complicit with consistently violates human rights and rights of nature around the world." (one of the judges 8 min in)
Initiating Actor Name: Bay Area Rights of Nature Alliance
Synopsis: In 2014, GARN held a people's tribunal that convened the Bay Area Rights of Nature Alliance to demonstrate the application of rights of nature laws.

Title: Danbury (USA) Town Ordinance: right to a sustainable energy future and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems...possess rights to exist and flourish within the Town of Danbury." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF, NH Community Rights, and Residents
Synopsis: A 2014 ordinance for Danbury, New Hampshire was revoked that would have banned unsustainable energy systems and recognized rights of nature.

Title: Gates Mills (USA) Town Charter: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems, including, but not limited to, woodlands, wetlands, streams, rivers, aquifers, and other water systems possess the rights to exist and flourish within Gates Mills." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2014, voters rejected a community bill of rights charter amendment for Gates Mills, Ohio that would have recognized rights of nature.

Title: Navajo Nation Tribal Code of 2002
Theoretical Framing: "All creation, from Mother Earth and Father Sky to the animals, those who live in water, those who fly and plant life have their own laws and have rights and freedoms to exist." (Page 21)
Initiating Actor Name: Honorable Edward T. Begay, the Speaker of the Navajo Nation Council, Mr. Henry Barber and Legislative Staff, and medicine people and elders
Synopsis: In 2002, the Navajo Nation adopted a Navajo Nation Code, which outlines the various rights and laws of the nation and recognizes rights of nature.

Title: Shrewsbury Township (USA) Ordinance: sewage sludge and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems possess inalienable and fundamental rights to exist and flourish within the Township of Shrewsbury." (Page 9)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2008, Shrewsbury, Pennsylvania adopted an ordinance that declares natural communities and ecosystems possess inalienable and fundamental rights.

Title: Spokane (USA) City Charter Amendment: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems, including but not limited to, all groundwater systems, surface water systems, and aquifers have the right to exist and flourish." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Envision Spokane
Synopsis: The court overturned a 2009 Community Bill of Rights recognizing rights of nature in Spokane, Washington.

Title: Randolph Township (USA) Resolution: oil extraction ban and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems, including, but not limited to, wetlands, streams, rivers, aquifers, and other water systems, possess inalienable and fundamental rights to exist and flourish within Randolph Township." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2012, a resolution banning unconventional shale and oil gas extraction and recognizing rights of nature was proposed for Randolph Township, Ohio.

Title: Highland Township (USA) Ordinance: hydraulic fracturing ban, community rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems, including, but not limited to, wetlands, streams, rivers, aquifers, and other water systems, possess inalienable and fundamental rights to exist and flourish within Highland Township." (Pages 2-3)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2013, a community bill of rights ordinance banning hydraulic fracturing waste dumping and recognizing rights of nature was overturned.

Title: Niles (USA) Town Ordinance: oil extraction ban, community rights, and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities possess the right to exist and flourish within the City." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and residents
Synopsis: A 2013 Niles, Ohio community rights-based ordinance banning new shale gas and oil extraction and recognizing rights of nature was overturned.

Title: Vermont State (USA) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "The natural environment of Vermont, including its forest, natural areas, surface and ground waters, and fish and wildlife populations, has certain natural, inherent inalienable rights to clean water and air, to health uncompromised by anthropogenic substances damaging to the systems of life, and to flourishing, connected habitats which supports the well-being of the flora and fauna of Vermont." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Steve Marx and Vermont Law School’s Earth Law Center and Vermont Rights of Nature
Synopsis: In 2012, the Vermont Rights of Nature Campaign drafted a proposed amendment to the Vermont Constitution giving nature legal rights.

Title: Crestone (USA) Town Ordinance: Dark Sky Community
Theoretical Framing: policy for implementing ecocentric law that protect natural systems based on their rights
Initiating Actor Name: International Dark Skies Association, Mayor Kairina Danforth
Synopsis: In 2020, the town of Crestone, Colorado approved an ordinance regulating light pollution for the health of humans and wildlife.

Title: Bowling Green (USA) Town Charter: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "The people of the City of Bowling Green, and the natural communities and ecosystems in Bowling Green, possess the right to a healthy environment and livable climate." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: The Bowling Green Climate Protectors
Synopsis: In 2017, a ballot initiative for a charter amendment for the city of Bowling Green in Ohio recognizing rights of nature and environmental rights failed.

Title: Malibu (USA) Proclamation: safe passage of whales and dolphins
Theoretical Framing: "Whales and dolphins are known to be highly intelligent and emotional creatures that live in families and other social groupings, associations that last for most, if not all, of their lives and therefore deserve the right to their own freedom and lives." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Malibu City Council
Synopsis: In 2014, the Malibu City Council passed a proclamation declaring the safe passage of whales and dolphins and that they deserve the right to their own freedom.

Title: Hebron (USA) Town Ordinance: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems...possess inalienable and fundamental rights to exist and flourish within the town of Hebron." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2014, residents of Herbron, New Hampshire adopted a community rights-based ordinance recognizing rights of nature and environmental rights.

Title: Athens (USA) Town Ordinance: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities possess the right to exist and flourish." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and residents
Synopsis: In 2012, Athens, Ohio adopted a Community Bill of Rights recognizing rights of nature and banning fracking within the county.

Title: Kent (USA) Town Charter: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems, including, but not limited to, wetlands, streams, rivers, aquifers, and other water systems, possess inalienable and fundamental rights to exist and flourish within the City of Kent." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Kent Environmental Rights Group and CELDF
Synopsis: In 2014, voters rejected a proposed amendment for Kent, Ohio's city charter recognizing rights of nature.

Title: Benton County (USA) Ordinance: local food systems bill of rights
Theoretical Framing:
"...the rights of natural communities and ecosystems to exist, persist, and flourish, and which promotes biodiversity, living soils, resilience, and nutrient density while providing for the social, equitable, economic, nutritional and cultural enhancements of the quality of life in Benton County." (Page 4)

"That right shall include the right to be free from genetically engineered organisms and the right to be free from the patenting, licensing, or ownership of their genes within Benton County." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Benton Food Freedom campaign
Synopsis: In 2015, an initiative to get an ordinance banning genetically modified organisms and recognizing rights of nature in Benton County, Oregon failed.

Title: Fulton County (USA) Charter Amendment: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems within the County of Fulton, including, but not limited to, rivers, streams, wetlands, and aquifers, possess the right to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve, free from activities prohibited by this Charter and other local enactments." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Fulton County Common Sense Energy Coalition and CELDF
Synopsis: In 2015, a court upheld a decision to keep a charter amendment recognizing rights of nature off of the ballot in Fulton County, Ohio.

Title: Medina County (USA) Charter Amendment: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems within the County of Medina, including, but not limited to, rivers, streams, wetlands, and aquifers, possess the right to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Sustainable Medina County
Synopsis: In 2017, the County Board of Elections refused to certify a petition for a new community charter that would have recognized rights of nature in Medina, Ohio.

Title: Mason (USA) Town Ordinance: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems in Mason possess rights to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2016, an ordinance recognizing rights of nature and the town's right to a sustainable energy future was submitted in Mason, New Hampshire.

Title: Barrington (USA) Town Ordinance: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems...possess rights to exist and flourish within the Town of Barrington." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2016, residents of Barrington, New Hampshire adopted a community rights-based ordinance recognizing rights of nature and environmental rights.

Title: Portage County (USA) Charter Amendment: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "All people of the County of Portage, along with ecosystems in the County (including, but not limited to, rivers, streams, wetlands, and aquifers), possess the right to clean air, water, and soil; to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve; and to be free from activities that would violate these rights." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Portage Community Rights Group/OCRN/CELDF
Synopsis: In 2016, an elections board declared a community charter in Portage, Ohio recognizing rights of nature and environmental rights unconstitutional.

Title: Waterville (USA) Town Charter: community bill of rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Natural communities and ecosystems, including, but not limited to, wetlands, streams, rivers, aquifers, and other water systems possess the rights to exist and flourish within the City of Waterville, including, but not limited to, the Maumee River." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Protecting Air for Waterville
Synopsis: In 2016, Waterville, Ohio passed a Waterville Community Bill of Rights Charter Amendment recognizing rights of nature.

Title: Ponca Nation (USA) Tribal Resolution: Rights of Nature
Theoretical Framing: "Each being has the right to a place and to play its role in Nature, which may include the responsible and ethical taking and consuming of living beings as is traditional, ethical, just and proper for sustenance and the perpetuation of life." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Ponca Tribe of Indians of Oklahoma
Synopsis: In 2017, the Ponca Nation passed a resolution recognizing rights of nature and creating penalties for crimes against nature.

Title: Ohio (USA) State Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: The amendment gives local communities the right to self government. "That right shall include the power of the people...to enact and enforce their local laws that protect their health, safety, and welfare by...establishing rights of...nature..." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF, Ohio Community Rights Network, and community members.
Synopsis: In 2019, a ballot initiative for a constitutional amendment for the state of Ohio recognizing the rights of nature failed.

Title: Ashland (USA) Town Ordinance: community rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems within Ashland possess the right to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF, Local residents
Synopsis: In 2018, an ordinance recognizing rights of nature and banning unsustainable energy projects in Ashland, New Hampshire did not pass.

Title: Newmarket (USA) Town Ordinance: community rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities within Newmarket possess the right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate, evolve, and be restored..." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: NHCR; Newmarket Citizen & Ecosystem Rights
Synopsis: In 2019, the council of Newmarket, New Hampshire voted not to adopt an ordinance recognizing rights of nature.

Title: Todd Township (USA) Ordinance: community rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities within Todd Township, including but not limited to rivers, streams, lakes, and aquifers, possess the right to exist, flourish, and evolve, naturally, which includes the right to be free from industrial farm activity as an infringement of this right." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Todd Township Community Action; CELDF
Synopsis: In 2018, a Community Bill of Rights ordinance banning industrial farming and recognizing rights of nature in Todd Township, Pennsylvania was overturned.

Title: Ecuador court case: rights of the Collay Protected Forest
Theoretical Framing: "Protección del Área de Bosque y Vegetación protectora del Collay y su respeto integral: El derecho de respetar de forma integral a la naturaleza, es una obligación de todas las personas particulares y del Estado, es decir la acción imperante de “respeto integral” a la naturaleza, la debemos todas las personas particulares y el Estado, hacia la naturaleza, nace de esta forma una relación jurídica con la naturaleza" (Page 19 - Second Instance)
Initiating Actor Name: Juan Diego Bustos (Mayor of the Gobierno Autónomo Descentralizado de Gualaceo); Romel Lucero Tacuri; Manuel Zhispon; Walter Garay Beltrán; María Muy Angamarca; y Rosa Angamarca Juca
Synopsis: In 2019, the Constitutional Court of Ecuador confirmed a ruling establishing violations to the rights of nature doing road work without environmental studies.

Title: Epping (USA) Town Resolution: right to self-government
Theoretical Framing: The people of Epping recognize their right to self-government "in an effort to protect the rights, health, safety, and welfare of all residents and ecosystems of Epping." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2019, the town of Epping, New Hampshire adopted a resolution giving the town the right to decide whether a liquified natural gas storage facility is allowed.

Title: Exeter (USA) Town Ordinance: community rights and rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Ecosystems and natural communities within Exter possess rights to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate, and evolve..." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and Residents
Synopsis: In 2019, Exeter, New Hampshire adopted an ordinance recognizing rights of nature and banning corporate activities releasing toxic waste.

Title: Oaxaca (Mexico) Court Case: protection of the Salado and Atoyac Rivers
Theoretical Framing: "Las autoridades responsables antes mencionadas de manera coordinada deberán: (...) Implementar acciones de conservación, restauración y vigilancia del equilibrio ecológico, así como la protección al ambiente." (Page 17 - First case)
Initiating Actor Name: Anonymous
Synopsis: In 2016, the Judiciary of the Mexican Federation granted a citizen's claim regarding the contamination of the Atoyac and Salados rivers.

Title: Pope Francis Holy See: Laudato Si (Care for our Common Home)
Theoretical Framing: "This implies a relationship of mutual responsibility between human beings and nature. Each community can take from the bounty of the earth whatever it needs  for subsistence, but it also has the duty to protect the earth and to ensure its fruitfulness for coming generations." (Page 49)
Initiating Actor Name: Pope Francis
Synopsis: In 2015, Pope Francis wrote an Encyclical Letter on ecocentrist values and the "duty to protect the Earth and to ensure its fruitfulness for coming generations"

Title: Universal Declaration of Animal Rights
Theoretical Framing: "All animals have equal rights to exist within the context of biological equilibrium." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: UNESCO
Synopsis: In 1978 at the UNESCO headquarters in Paris, UNESCO proclaimed the Universal Declaration of Animal Rights.

Title: Global Pact for the Environment 2017
Theoretical Framing: "The Parties shall take necessary measures to maintain and restore the diversity and capacity of ecosystems and human communities to withstand environmental disruptions and degradation and to recover and adapt." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Proposed by an international network of jurists coordinated by Environment Commission of the Club des Juristes
Synopsis: The Global Pact was presented to states by an international network of hundreds of experts to develop a global legal framework that protects the planet.

Title: UN General Assembly Resolution (2020): Harmony with Nature
Theoretical Framing: "Recognizes that protecting and conserving ecosystems and avoiding harmful practices against animals, plants, microorganisms and non-living environments contributes to the coexistence of humankind in harmony with nature." (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: United Nations
Synopsis: In 2020, the UN General Assembly adopted a resolution promoting harmony with nature.

Title: UN Secretary-General Report (2020): Harmony with Nature
Theoretical Framing: "Responsibility now lies with the Organization to be the champion of non-anthropocentrism and a voice on behalf of the natural world and to play a lead role for a twenty-first century global Earth-centred transition, in which the lives of all human and non-human species matter." (Page 15)

Title: Mexico Supreme Court Case: cockfighting ban
Theoretical Framing: "Esta Primera Sala considera que la protección del bienestar de los animales es una finalidad que puede limitar de manera legítima los derechos fundamentales de los quejosos. Theoretical Framing: "First I want to point out what are the claims of the plaintiff, that is, the violation of the rights of nature, to maintain their life cycles, as well as the obvious violation of the right to water, the right to health, the right to food sovereignty, the right to live in a healthy and ecologically balanced environment and the right to good living and consequently, they request the following as a measure of reparation" (Page 8 of English translation of municipal court judgement Part 2).
