That the watersheds feed and be fed by rainfall, snowmelt, and aquifers; d. That biodiversity is maintained; and e. That the predominantly free-flowing nature of the South St. Vrain Creek is maintained.” (2)
Initiating Actor Name: Town of Lyons Board of Trustees; Lyons Ecology Advisory Board; Save the Colorado (NGO)
Ecological Actor Name: St. Vrain Creek watersheds and ecosystems
Synopsis: On November 20, 2023, the town of Lyons, Colorado adopted a Rights-of-Nature like resolution to protect the St. Vrain Creek watersheds and their ecosystems.

Title: Rugvin Foundation (Netherlands) Nature Governance: Sedna the porpoise on the board
Location: North Sea and Oosterschelde Estuary, Netherlands
Theoretical Framing: "As a board, we have decided that we want to pay more attention to the global development of the " rights of nature " by giving a voice to Sedna the porpoise, who will not only represent her peers on the board, but also the entire marine biodiversity in seas and oceans." (Website)
Initiating Actor Name: The Rugvin Foundation
Synopsis: In February 2024, Sedna the Porpoise joined the board of the Rugvin Foundation, a Dutch NGO that researches and monitors whale species in the North Sea.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 8th Local Hearing: Tren Maya 2023
Theoretical Framing: "recognize in an irrefutable way the violation of the Rights of Nature and the biocultural rights of the Mayan People, who have been and continue to be protectors and guardians of their territory. Of their cenotes, caves and coasts; their jungles, their biodiversity and traditional crops, and the non-human beings that inhabit their ecosystems, all of which constitute crimes of ecocide and ethnocide. The Tribunal holds the Mexican State responsible for the violation of these fundamental rights of Nature and of the Maya People." (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: International Tribunal for the Rights of Nature; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN); Asamblea de Defensores del Territorio Maya Múuch' Xíinbal; Consejo Civil Mexicano para la Silvicultura Sostenible
Synopsis: In March 2023, the International Rights of Nature Tribunal held it's 8th local tribunal in Yucatan, Mexico on the Tren Maya (Mayan Train) megaproject.

Title: Quebec (Canada) Law: rights of the Saint Lawrence River
Theoretical Framing: "The purpose of this bill is to strengthen protection of the St. Lawrence River. To that end, the bill creates a special legal status for the St. Lawrence River by conferring on it several rights, including the rights to protection, to conservation and to restoration. The bill establishes a council of guardians under the name “Conseil des gardiens du Fleuve”, whose mandate is to protect the interests of the River. The Council is composed of seven members called “guardians of the River”, including two from Indigenous nations, who are, among other things, responsible for representing the River before the courts."
Initiating Actor Name: Émilise Lessard-Therrien (Québec Solidaire political party)
Synopsis: On May 5, 2022, a bill was introduced in the National Assembly of Quebec to recognize the rights and special legal status of the Saint Lawrence River.

Title: Serra de Outes (Spain) Declaration: rights of the Tins River
Theoretical Framing: "Na sesión de gobernanza “O Futuro do río Tins”, organizada na Casa da Cultura da Serra de Outes o 3 de marzo de 2023, o medio cento de persoas participantes, representando numerosas entidades e grupos de interese do concello, valoraron a importancia que para cada persoa e colectivo tiñan distintas facetas do río Tins para a súa vida e a das comunidades ribeirás: como espazo e fonte de auga limpa; como foco e corredor de biodiversidade; como fonte de vida e chave do ciclo hidrolóxico; como espazo con ribeiras arboradas e vivas; como lugar de festas e celebracións; como vínculo coa ría e o seu esteiro; como suxeito de dereitos; como espazo histórico e de patrimonio cultural; como lugar de paz, beleza e tranquilidade" (1)
Initiating Actor Name: Serra de Outes city council; Centro de Estudios Eurorregionales Galicia-Norte de Portugal (CEER) Foundation; Montescola Foundation; Universidad de Santiago; Universidad da Coruña; and Universidad de Vigo
Synopsis: In March 2024, Serra de Outes in Galicia adopted a Declaration of the Rights of the Tins River—making it the first river in Spain to have its rights recognized.

Title: Guajará-Mirim (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Art. 126. The Municipality shall provide, with the effective participation of the population, for the preservation, conservation, defence, recovery and improvement of the natural, artificial and working environment, taking into account regional and local peculiarities, to ensure that all members of nature, whether human or not, have the right to an ecologically healthy and balanced environment, in harmony with the social and ecological-economic needs of human beings." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Councillor Francisco Oro Waram
Synopsis: On March 15, 2023, the municipality of Guajará-Mirim, Brazil adopted an amendment to its Organic Law recognizing the Rights of Nature.

Title: José de Freitas (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "To this end, concrete actions are needed to defend nature's right to be a subject of rights. The contemporary socio-ecological crisis, which generates social inequality, poverty and the degradation of natural resources, has generated interest in the community in general to discuss economic development and the environment." (Page 4)
Initiating Actor Name: Councillor Yarema Leite Rodrigues de Sousa Alves (Solidarity Party); Mariana Ribeiro Santiago, Ian Cavalcante, and Igor Miranda de Carvalho (lawyers)
Synopsis: On 11 July 2023, the municipality of José de Freitas, Brazil approved an amendment to its Organic Law to recognize the rights of nature.

Title: Cáceres (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Protecting the rights of nature is a fundamental issue in guaranteeing the sustainability and survival of planet Earth" "Nature and human beings are interconnected and interdependent. Without a healthy and sustainable environment, human beings cannot survive. It is therefore our duty to protect and preserve nature." "Nature has intrinsic value, i.e. value in itself. It should not just be seen as a resource for human benefit. We have an ethical responsibility to protect and respect nature for its own sake." (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: Municipal Councillor Cézare Pastorello; civil society and NGOs (see participants bellow)
Synopsis: In August 2023, the Municipality of Cáceres in Brazil repealed an amendment to include the Rights of Nature in its municipal Organic Law.

Title: Alagoa Nova (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "The Public Prosecutor's Office, the Public Defender's Office and any association whose institutional aims include the protection of public and social heritage, the environment, the rights of racial, ethnic or religious groups or cultural, artistic, aesthetic, historical, tourist and landscape heritage, have active legitimacy to demand compliance with the Rights of Nature and its elements from the Public Authorities by means of appropriate action." (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: Municipal Council of Alagoa Nova
Synopsis: On 10 October 2023, the Municipality of Alagoa Nova, Brazil approved an amendment to its Organic Law recognizing the rights of nature.

Title: Alto Paraguai (Brazil) Municipal Organic Law: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Thus, the city of Alto Paraguai-MT, which forms part of the important Paraguai River Basin, requires respect and harmony with nature centred on a vision of the Earth, justice and the good life of contemporary society, in line with the Harmony of Nature platform and its ownership and innate right to exist." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: Mariana Ribeiro Santiago, Éverton Neves dos Santos, and Alessandro de Almeida Santana Souza
Synopsis: In December 2023, the municipality of Alto Paraguai in Brazil approved an amendment to its Organic Law to recognize the rights of nature and Harmony with Nature

Title: Minas Gerais State (Brazil) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "This proposed amendment to the Constitution aims to add art. 214-A to the Constitution of the State of Minas Gerais, establishing full and concrete rights to nature, imposing on the public authorities and the community the duty to defend it, ensure its recovery, protection and the maintenance of the fauna, flora and other ecological, biological, genetic and biogeochemical processes, ensuring the rights of nature to prosper and evolve, and in harmony with the cultural processes of human life, for the benefit of current and future generations, human and non-human" (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: state representative Leninha (Workers Party)
Synopsis: On February 2, 2023, an amendment was proposed to the state constitution of Minas Gerais, Brazil recognizing the Rights of Nature and the pursuit of 'bem viver'

Title: Paraíba State (Brazil) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "Art. 227-Nature, where life is also reproduced and realised, has full, intrinsic and perpetual rights, inherent to its existence on the planet, imposing on the public authorities and the community the duty to defend it, ensure its recovery, protection and the maintenance of fauna, flora and other ecological, biological, genetic and biogeochemical processes, ensuring the rights of nature to thrive and evolve, and in harmony coexist with the cultural processes of human life, for the benefit of current and future generations, human and civil." (Page 1) "X-attributes to the Caatinga the qualification of state heritage, for its intrinsic rights, whose interventions, in the form of the law, should occur within conditions that ensure conservation and restoration, in respect for the principles of the rights of Nature and harmony with Nature, preserving the ecological balance of the environment" (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Representative Estela Bezerra (Workers Party); Vanessa Hasson, MAPAS (NGO)
Synopsis: On October 11, 2022, representative Estela Bezerra proposed an amendment to the State of Paraíba constitution, recognizing the Rights of Nature.

Title: Santa Catarina State (Brazil) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "This Constitution Amendment Proposal changes the article 181 of the Constitution of the State of Santa Catarina, adding two paragraphs (1st and 2nd) that recognize the inherent rights of nature, aiming to remove anthropocentrism from the cognitive center of State’s initiatives and from the society as a whole, putting in place life, human and non-human." (Page 2) "this proposal aims to include in the legal and institutional order of the State of Santa Catarina, the promotion of Earth, this living organism, containing humans and non humans, and to put on the agenda an important constitutional norm: the defense of life." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: Congressman Father Pedro Baldissera (Workers' Party)
Synopsis: On April 20, 2022, congressman Pedro Baldissera proposed an amendment to the State of Santa Catarina (Brazil) constitution, recognizing the Rights of Nature.

Title: Grant Township (USA) Home Rule Charter 2015
Theoretical Framing: "Section 104. All residents of Grant Township, along with natural communities and ecosystems within the Township, possess the right to clean air, water, and soil, which shall include the right to be free from activities which may pose potential risks to clean air, water, and soil within the Township, including the depositing of waste from oil and gas extraction."
Initiating Actor Name: CELDF and residents
Synopsis: Grant Township's Home Rule charter declares that all residents, natural communities and ecosystems in the town "possess the right to clean air, water, and soil"

Title: Bahia State (Brazil) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "The cultural relationship with nature is validated through all the social benefits achieved by balanced ecosystems, making it possible to maintain the ancestry of a people and the relationships established with the planet, such as forms of expression, ways of creating, doing and living, through the balanced relationship of nature with native peoples and traditional communities." (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: State of Bahia Deputy Hilton Coelho (PSOL-BA)
Synopsis: On December 5, 2023, Hilton Coelho proposed an amendment to the Constitution of Bahia to recognize the Rights of Nature.

Title: Hawaii State (USA) Law: rights and legal personhood of watersheds
Theoretical Framing: "Any watershed located in the State shall be recognized as a legal person and shall have the rights, powers, and duties of a legal person, including the right to exist, flourish, and naturally evolve, as reflected by native Hawaiian traditional and customary cultural values, practice, and woridview, including the notion and practice of malama ‘ama." (6)
Initiating Actor Name: Representative Kirstin Kahaloa; legislature of the state of Hawaii
Synopsis: In January 2024, Hawaii introduced a bill to recognize legal personhood and rights of watersheds, in reflection of native Hawaiian traditional beliefs.

Title: He Whakaputanga Moana (Whale Protection and Legal Personhood Declaration)
Theoretical Framing: recognizes whales as legal persons with inherent rights, including the right to freedom of movement, a healthy environment, and the ability to thrive alongside humanity. It draws upon the traditions of Te Ao Māori (Māori worldview) and emphasizes the interconnectedness of all living things. Recognizing the urgent threats whales face from unsustainable practices, pollution, and climate change, the declaration outlines a comprehensive plan for their protection. He Whakaputanga Moana aims to establish marine protected areas and implement rāhui (customary restrictions guided by ancestral wisdom). The declaration also advocates for weaving together mātauranga Māori (Indigenous knowledge) with science for a more holistic approach to whale protection. The declaration emphasizes the crucial role of Polynesian communities as kaitiaki (guardians) and proposes the creation of the Hinemoana Halo Ocean Protection Fund that will empower Indigenous communities as the natural guardians, and support initiatives aligned with the declaration’s goals.
Initiating Actor Name: Hinemoana Halo Ocean Initiative; Maori and Pacific Indigenous leaders
Synopsis: In March 2024, Pacific Indigenous leaders ratified the He Whakaputanga Moana (Declaration for the Ocean), which recognizes whales as legal persons with rights.

Title: Minnesota (USA) State Resolution: rights of manoomin
Theoretical Framing: "A resolution recognizing Wild Rice (Manoomin in Anishinaabe and Psíŋ in Dakota and herein referred as Manoomin/Psíŋ) as sacred and central to the culture and health of Indigenous Peoples in Minnesota and critical to the health and identity of all Minnesota citizens and ecosystems; and establishing a commitment to passing Rights of Manoomin/Psíŋ legislation to protect wild rice and the freshwater resources upon which it depends"
Initiating Actor Name: Representative Jamie Becker Finn & Senator Mary Kunesh; Minnesota Interfaith Power & Light (NGO); Rise & Repair alliance
Synopsis: On April 4, 2024, a resolution was introduced in Minnesota state legislature to recognize the inherent rights of Manoomin/Psíŋ (Wild Rice).

Title: India Supreme Court Case: State of Telangana v. Mohd. Abdul Qasim
Theoretical Framing: “The time has come for mankind to live sustainably and respect the rights of rivers, lakes, beaches, estuaries, ridges, trees, mountains, seas and air. It is imperative to do so as there is always a constant threat to forests due to the ever increasing population. Man is bound by nature’s law. Therefore, the need of the hour is to transform from an anthropocentric approach to ecocentric approach which will encompass a wider perspective in the interest of the environment.” (pg. 32)
Initiating Actor Name: Justice (J. M.M. Sundresh)
Synopsis: In 2024, an Indian Supreme Court considered ecocentrism, rights to nature and climate change in a ruling in favor of a protected forest in Kompally.

Title: Mexico State (Mexico) Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "In the State of Mexico, Nature or biodiversity, endemic and native species are subjects of rights, which are granted, protected and promoted by the Constitution and the laws of the State of Mexico, at the same level as human rights.” (Article 5)
Initiating Actor Name: Deputy Max Agustín Correa Hernández (Morena Party)
Synopsis: In April 2024, the State of Mexico approved an amendment to its local Constitution recognizing the rights of nature and biodiversity.

Title: Germany court case: seals in the North Sea (1988)
Theoretical Framing: "The animal is therefore still a thing in the sense of civil and administrative law; it is not somehow elevated to legal personality." The seals' "existential need to survive" was the starting point for the lawsuit: because it is "about the continued existence of an entire animal species".
Initiating Actor Name: Students of Hamburg
Synopsis: In 1988, for the first time, "seals in the North Sea" sued the Federal Republic of Germany over dumping of toxic waste in the North Sea.

Title: Sounds Right (International) music initiative: nature as an official artist
Theoretical Framing: "SOUNDS RIGHT IS A GROUNDBREAKING MUSIC MOVEMENT. IT UNITES PEOPLE AROUND THE WORLD IN A SHARED COMMITMENT, RECOGNIZING THE INTRINSIC VALUE OF NATURE. TOGETHER, WE MUST ACT NOW TO PROTECT OUR PLANET FOR OUR COMMON FUTURE." (Melissa Fleming, UN Under-Secretary-General for Global Communication)..."NATURE’s royalties and donations will be collected by EarthPercent, a registered charity in England and Wales (1188391) and a registered 501(c)3 nonprofit foundation in the United States (EIN: 86-3830611). EarthPercent will distribute these funds to rights-based NATURE conservation and restoration projects in the world’s most precious and precarious ecosystems under the guidance of the Sounds Right Expert Advisory Panel, a group of world-leading biologists, environmental activists, Indigenous People’s representatives, and experts in conservation funding. The Panel will consider projects with proven models of ecological and community impact, robust monitoring and evaluation frameworks, and delivered by organisations with the right capabilities. Projects in ecosystems with the highest levels of biodiversity and endemism will be prioritised, with initial analysis identifying Key Biodiversity Areas in the following regions: Madagascar and the Indian Ocean Islands; Indo-Burma, India, and Myanmar; Sundaland; Philippines; Tropical Andes; and, the Atlantic Forest."
Initiating Actor Name: Sounds Right (Museum for the United Nations – UN Live)
Synopsis: In 2024, Nature became recognized as an official artist on streaming platforms, allowing Nature to be officially credited for it's sounds used in recorded music

Title: Nijmegen (Netherlands) Municipal Motion: rights for nature in the city
Theoretical Framing: "the recognition of rights for nature entails a society in harmony with nature, where people and nature form one inseparable whole with the ecosystem; Granting legal personality to nature can improve attention to and protection of our natural environment and stimulate innovative approaches to sustainability"
Initiating Actor Name: Eline Lauret (Partij voor de Dieren)
Synopsis: In April 2024, a 'Rights for Nature' motion was submitted to the Nijmegen city council in the Netherlands to recognize rights and legal personality for Nature.

Title: North Carolina State (USA) Law: rights of the Dan River ecosystem
Theoretical Framing: "This act recognizes and protects the rights of the Dan River ecosystem and the right of the people of North Carolina to a healthy, thriving Dan River ecosystem." (page 1) "The Dan River ecosystem possesses rights, including, but not limited to, all of the following: (1) The right to naturally exist, flourish, regenerate, and evolve. (2) The right to full restoration, recovery, and preservation. (3) The right to abundant, pure, clean, unpolluted water, including the right to natural surface water flow and recharge and groundwater recharge (4) The right to a healthy natural environment and natural biodiversity. (5) The right to carry on all natural functions of the Dan River ecosystem. (6) The right to be free of activities, practices, and any other man-made obstructions that interfere with or infringe upon the rights set forth in this section. (page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Representative Pricey Harrison; 7 Directions of Service (NGO)
Synopsis: May 1, 2024, a bill was introduced in North Carolina’s state legislature to grant legal rights to the Dan River, including the right to be free from pollution.

Title: Finland citizens’ initiative: constitutional proposal for animal rights
Theoretical Framing: "Animals have legal standing" (Section 1). "Public authorities must safeguard the realisation of fundamental animal rights and develop society in a way, which guarantees the fundamental rights of animals. Companies must respect fundamental animal rights in their activities" (Section 2). "A wild animal has the right to life and the right to live in freedom, in the animal’s natural habitat" (Section 3).
Initiating Actor Name: Finnish Association of Animal Rights Lawyers (NGO); citizens of Finland
Synopsis: December 2023, a citizen's initiative was submitted to the Finnish parliament that proposes adding animal rights to Finlands constitution alongside human rights

Title: Goiás (Brazil) Municipal Law: rights of the Vermelho River
Theoretical Framing: Rights of the Vermelho River: To maintain its natural flow and in sufficient quantity to guarantee the health of the ecosystem; To nourish and be nourished by the surrounding forests and endemic biodiversity; To exist with adequate physical and chemical conditions for its ecological balance; To interrelate with human beings through biocultural identification, spiritual practices, leisure, artisanal fishing, agroecology and culture.
Initiating Actor Name: Councilwoman Elenízia da Mata de Jesus (PT)
Synopsis: On June 13, 2024, the Goiás City Council in Brazil unanimously approved a bill granting legal rights to the Vermelho River in Brazil.

Title: Linhares (Brazil) Municipal Law: rights of the waves
Theoretical Framing: In summary, the waves are entitled to: Maintaining their physical-chemical conditions for ecological balance; Protecting water bodies in the ecosystem to prevent human interference with vital ecological cycles; Foster harmonious interactions with humans through cultural, spiritual, leisure, and ecological practices; Representation by stakeholders with a special connection to the waves' ecological cycle in all relevant public decision-making processes, and support from governmental, community, and civil society groups in public decision-making processes; Integration of traditional knowledge and conservation practices into decision-making while balancing them with scientific approaches; Ensuring accountability and rectification for human-caused damages to their recognized intrinsic rights.
Initiating Actor Name: Municipal Council of Linhares; Regencia Surfing Association; MAPAS (NGO)
Ecological Actor Name: Waves of the Doce River Mouth
Synopsis: In June 2024, the municipal council of Linhares in Brazil approved a bill that recognizes the waves at the mouth of the Doce River as entities with rights.

Title: Ecuador Court Case: rights of the Machángara River
Theoretical Framing: The court recognized that since the river is alive, it is subject to rights under the Constitution of Ecuador, which establishes that nature possesses a right to protection, promotion, and restoration. The Constitutional Court of Ecuador previously recognized that rivers are protected under Chapter 7 of the Constitution in 2022.
Initiating Actor Name: Cabildo Cívico de Quito; Kitu Kara Indigenous people
Synopsis: In July 2024, a court in Ecuador ruled that the Machángara River is a subject of rights and the municipality of Quito has breeched said rights.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 12th Local Hearing: Yesah 2024
Theoretical Framing: "The Yesah Tribunal recognizes the inherent Rights of Rivers and their ecosystems, the inherent Rights of Indigenous Peoples and the inherent Rights of Nature...10. As Indigenous People, The Yesah Tribunal recognizes that what happens to Mother Earth happens to us all," (Page 5)
Initiating Actor Name: International Rights of Nature Tribunal; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN); 7 Directions of Service; Movement Rights;
Synopsis: In June 2024, the International Rights of Nature Tribunal held its 12th local hearing, the Yesah Tribunal brought against the Mountain Valley Pipeline (MVP).

Title: Brazil Constitutional Amendment: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "CHAPTER VI THE RIGHTS OF NATURE, Art. 1 Nature is recognized as a subject of rights and is made up of all living beings, human and non-human, that inhabit it and depend on it for their survival. Art. 2 The beings that make up Nature are recognized with the rights to protect their lives, ecosystems and preservation by promoting the balance of their interrelationships. Art. 3 The ancestral and historical relationship of indigenous peoples and other traditional peoples and communities with the preservation of Nature is recognized, as well as the maintenance of these relationships as a guaranteed right to maintain the ways of life of these populations."
Initiating Actor Name: Representative Célia Xakriabá
Synopsis: In January 2025, a proposed amendment to the Brazilian Federal Constitution (PEC) was submitted to the National Congress to recognize the Rights of Nature.

Title: Black Mountain (USA) Ordinance: Rights of the Swannanoa River
Theoretical Framing: "the inherent rights of the Swannanoa River Watershed Ecosystem to thrive, regenerate, and flourish. The land within which the Town of Black Mountain is now chartered is unceded territory of Cherokee and Catawba peoples. We are proposing to codify into law our rights to restore and protect our watershed from the systemic threats and irreparable harm to its ecological integrity, an approach rooted in Indigenous values which regard land and beings as sacred heritage and living legacy." (2)
Initiating Actor Name: Katherine Winship; Kevin Jones; Emily Kerscher; Community Environment Legal Defense Fund (CELDF)
Synopsis: In 2024, residents of Black Mountain, North Carolina drafted a proposed amendment to the Town Charter to recognize the rights of the Swannanoa River.

Title: Nigeria River Ethiope Rights Act
Theoretical Framing: These rights include: the right to flow unimpeded, maintain its natural biodiversity, protection from pollution, restoration when harmed, the right to be heard in court, and the appointment of guardians to enforce its rights.
Initiating Actor Name: River Ethiope Trust Foundation (RETFON); Earth Law Center
Synopsis: In 2019, the Earth Law Center and River Ethiope Trust created the River Ethiope Rights Act – a draft law to grant legal rights for the River Ethiope in Nigeria.

Title: Colombia Law: rights of the Rancheria River
Theoretical Framing: "The purpose of this Law is to declare the Ranchería River, its basin and its tributaries, as an entity subject to rights for its conservation, maintenance and restoration by the State and with the effective participation of the communities that live in the area of ​​influence of the Ranchería River, in the department of La Guajira." (1)
Initiating Actor Name: Congress of Colombia
Synopsis: On August 8, 2024, the Congress of Colombia passed a law declaring the Rancheria River a subject of rights.

Title: Colombia court case: rights of jaguars, macaws, and tropical dry forest
Location: Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta region, Colombia
Theoretical Framing: A new lawsuit asks Colombia to recognize that jaguars, military macaws and the nation’s tropical dry forest have legal rights to “life, health and integrity.”
Initiating Actor Name: Amar Madre Tierra Foundation (NGO)
Synopsis: In July 2024, a lawsuit was filed in Colombian court to recognize the rights of jaguars, macaws, and the nation’s tropical dry forest.

Title: First Nations and Tribes Treaty: Grizzly Bear Protection
Theoretical Framing: "The Grizzly: A Treaty of Cooperation, Cultural Revitalization and Restoration"
Initiating Actor Name: Piikani Nation Chief and Council of the Blackfoot Confederacy
Synopsis: In September 2016, First Nations and Tribes across North America signed a cross-boarder Indigenous Treaty to protect the sacred Grizzly Bear.

Title: Rappahannock Tribe Constitution: rights of the Rappahannock River
Theoretical Framing: “exist, flourish, regenerate, and evolve.” This includes rights to groundwater recharge, to support healthy habitats for native species, and to maintain natural water flow.
Initiating Actor Name: Rappahannock Tribe; Indigenous Conservation Council for the Chesapeake Bay (ICC); Center for Democratic and Environmental Rights (CDER)
Synopsis: On October 2024, the Rappahannock Tribe in Virginia became the first tribal nation in the U.S. to adopt a Tribal Constitution recognizing the rights of nature.

Title: Groningen (Netherlands) Initiative Proposal: rights for nature in the city
Theoretical Framing: "Giving nature a voice can be done in different ways: by giving nature rights, or by appointing a guardian to stand up for nature...This initiative proposal asks to start the discussion about a voice for nature in the Municipality of Groningen and to investigate together with residents for which areas in the municipality this could be a good solution in order to ensure that nature in Groningen can flourish and biodiversity can be strengthened." (2). "There are two possibilities, the legal line where a nature area is given legal personality, and a softer line where only a chair is put at the table for a guardian. In the case of legal personality, nature itself can go to court to defend its interests. So not a nature organization, but the nature area itself. A guardian can defend its rights on behalf of nature. A combination of these is also possible." (5)
Initiating Actor Name: Party for the Animals; D66; GroenLinks; Stichting Rechten van de Natuur
Synopsis: In May 2025, an initiative proposal was passed in the Municipality of Groningen, Netherlands to investigate whether nature can be granted rights.

Title: Erfurt (Germany) Regional Court Case: rights of nature via the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights (first ruling)
Theoretical Framing: "The Charter provides the comprehensive right of ecological persons to have their existence, preservation and regeneration of their life cycles, structure, functions and development processes respected and protected.” (section 30). "The recognition of specific rights of ecological persons through the interpretation and application of existing Union law is necessary due to the importance and urgency of the ecological challenges - climate change, species extinction and global littering - and in view of the threat of irreversible damage. The Charter's preamble emphasises responsibility and duties towards other people, the human community and future generations. (Section 32-33)
Initiating Actor Name: Judge Dr Martin Borowsky
Synopsis: In August 2024, the District Court of Erfurt in Germany set a legal precedent by recognizing the rights of nature in a court ruling.

Title: Peru constitutional court case: rights of the wild andean fox “Run Run”
Theoretical Framing: The court reasoned that animals, like humans, are sentient, thinking, and conscious beings, and should be respected and protected under the law. As such, Run Run was provided protection, rights, and autonomous guardianship as a “member of nature,” derived from the concept of rights of nature. This decision is based on the theory of sentient beings, which recognizes animals as capable of experiencing feelings and possessing intrinsic dignity. However, the protection of animals based on animal welfare, and because they are sentient, does not imply granting rights. Therefore, the regulatory framework of the Rights of Nature was crucial in this decision, which allows non-human beings to have rights.
Initiating Actor Name: Supreme Court of Justice of Lima, Third Constitutional Court; Peruvian Institute of Legal Advice for the Environment and Biodiversity (Ipalema)
Synopsis: In June 2024, Peru's Constitutional Court granted rights to a wild Andean fox, and ruled that the state must respect the rights of nature, including animals.

Title: Switzerland court case: voting rights of wild bees and minor children
Theoretical Framing: The case considered whether or not wild bees and minor children should be given the political right to vote on the proposed biodiversity initiative. The minor based her claim on the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child, especially Comment No. 26 (2023) Children's rights and the environment. The girl from Switzerland argued that the right to a clean, healthy and sustainable environment as it is safeguarded in the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child includes the rights of nature, and therefore, also voting rights for herself and the Swiss wild bee population. The Government Council of the Canton of Zurich and the Swiss Federal Supreme Cout rejected the complaint, stating that wild bees are not holders of fundamental rights and are therefore excluded from the scope of political rights.
Initiating Actor Name: Swiss citizen (child) and wild bees
Synopsis: An 11-year-old girl and wild bees claim voting rights to protect nature in Switzerland; Federal Supreme Court rejects, case goes to UN Child Rights Committee.

Title: Lucerne (Switzerland) Constitutional Amendment: Rights of the Reuss River
Theoretical Framing:
Section 10 (3) (new)
The fundamental rights and legal personality of non-human nature are guaranteed in accordance with the cantonal constitution.

Section 10bis Fundamental rights and legal personality of water bodies (new)

1 The public water bodies of the canton are endowed with fundamental rights in accordance with
paragraph 2 and legal personality.
2 Water bodies have the right to exist and to ecological integrity.
3 The law regulates the practical implementation. It shall be designed in such a way as to ensure the effective and independent assertion and enforcement of the rights granted.
Initiating Actor Name: Rechtsperson Reuss; Markus Schärli (founder of Rechtsperson Reuss)
Synopsis: In January 2026, the required signatures were submitted to the Lucerne government, to recognize River Reuss and other public water bodies as legal persons.

Title: St. Gallen (Switzerland) court case: hunting permits and the rights of wolves
Location: Calfeisen valley, St. Gallen Canton, Switzerland
Theoretical Framing: claimed that the wolves have a right to appeal and to judicial procedures in their own right. This claim was based on Art. 2 paragraph 4 and Art. 120 paragraph 2 of the Swiss Constitution, as well as Art. 5 of the UN Convention on Animal Health and Protection Draft (UNCAPH) of the Global Animal Law Association (GAL).
Initiating Actor Name: Wolves of the Calfeisental Pack and Citizen
Synopsis: In 2024, a citizen filed an appeal against a hunting permit for wolves, claiming the wolves have a right to judicial procedures in their own right.

Title: Democratic Republic of Congo Declaration: international crime of ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "when there is a violation of ecosystems, for those responsible to be punished and held accountable for their actions, and for the destruction of ecosystems to be made a crime recognised under Article 7 of the Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court." "By supporting this initiative, we protect our natural heritage and send a clear message that environmental crimes will not go unpunished.” (DRC Minister of the Environment, Eve Bazaiba)
Initiating Actor Name: DRC Minister of the Environment, Eve Bazaiba
Synopsis: October 31, 2024, the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) publicly declared its support for establishing ecocide as an international crime.

Title: Vanuatu, Fiji, and Samoa Proposal: ecocide amendment to the Rome Statute
Theoretical Framing: "Addition to Article 5: “(e) The crime of ecocide.” Addition of Article 8 ter: “Article 8 ter Ecocide 1. For the purpose of this Statute, “ecocide” means unlawful or wanton acts committed with knowledge that there is a substantial likelihood of severe and either widespread or long-term damage to the environment being caused by those acts." (3)
Initiating Actor Name: The Republic of Vanuatu, Fiji, and Samoa
Synopsis: September 9, 2024, Vanuatu, Fiji and Samoa formally submitted a proposed amendment to the Rome Statute to include a crime of “ecocide”.

Title: Everett (USA) Ordinance: rights of the Snohomish River
Theoretical Framing: "A. Rights of the Snohomish River Watershed. The Snohomish River Watershed possesses the rights to exist, regenerate, and flourish, which shall include the right to naturally recharge, the right to naturally flow, the right to water quality necessary to provide habitat for native plants and animals, the right to provide clean water, and the right to restoration. The Snohomish River Watershed shall also have the right to be free from activities or projects which violate those rights."
Initiating Actor Name: Standing for the Snoho
Synopsis: In November 2024, voters in Everett, Washington approved a ballot initiative that grants the Snohomish River legal rights to “exist, regenerate, and flourish”.

Title: Resistencia (Argentina) Court Case: rights of Laguna Francia
Theoretical Framing: "In the case of the Francia-Altabe Lagoon, which is an area of ​​high ecological sensitivity and affected by human activities that compromise its capacity for water regulation and biological diversity, a biocentric approach must be adopted. Recognizing it as a subject of rights not only allows its protection against activities that can degrade it, but also requires the implementation of measures to reverse the environmental damage already caused, respecting the principles of prevention, precaution and intergenerational equity established in the General Environmental Law (Law No. 25,675) and reaffirmed in the Escazú Agreement."
Initiating Actor Name: Civil and Commercial Court No. 21 of Resistencia
Synopsis: In 2024, the Municipal Court of Resistencia in Chaco, Argentina ruled that the Laguna Francia is a subject of rights.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 14th Local Hearing: Serbia 2024
Theoretical Framing: "...we ask that you consider the totality of our complaint based upon the Rights of Nature. In making this analysis, note that oftentimes Nature is determined to possess, at minimum, the rights to exist, thrive, and evolve naturally.."
Initiating Actor Name: International Rights of Nature Tribunal; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN); Zoe Lujic; Earth Thrive; Balkan Centre for the Rights of Nature
Synopsis: In November 2024, the International Rights of Nature Tribunal convened its 14th local hearing on gold mining in Homolje, Serbia and severe ecological damage.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 13th Local Hearing: Corrientes 2024
Theoretical Framing: "This trial not only represents a complaint, but also an urgent call to rethink the management of the territory in Corrientes and to seek productive alternatives that prioritize the preservation of ecosystems and the well-being of the communities."
Initiating Actor Name: International Rights of Nature Tribunal; Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN); Defensores del Pastizal
Synopsis: In November 2024, the International Tribunal on the Rights of Nature traveled to Corrientes to conduct a hearing on the case of monoculture plantations.

Title: Chile Citizen Declaration: rights of the Biobío River
Theoretical Framing: "Reaffirming the profound connection between the waters of the Biobío River and the spirituality of the Mapuche Pewenche and Lafkenche peoples. The worldview of these Indigenous communities emphasizes the necessity of managing their waters in a sustainable and respectful manner, recognizing their intrinsic value. Consequently, the Biobío River stands not only as a cultural, historical, scenic, and recreational resource for the inhabitants of the basin and the nation as a whole, but also as a symbol of cultural identity and a witness to the history of local communities."
Initiating Actor Name: Pewenches and Lafkenches Indigenous communities; Defensa Ambiental
Synopsis: In September 2024, the Biobío River becomes the first ecosystem in Chile to have a Declaration of Rights, recognizing its rights to exist, flow, and regenerate.

Title: Erfurt (Germany) Regional Court Case: inherent rights of nature in calculating damages(second ruling)
Theoretical Framing: the court considered the “inherent rights of nature”—deriving them from the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights (Para. 30.) Although the plaintiff did not raise this point, the district court on its own accord determined it should take into account the inherent rights of nature involved in this case.
Initiating Actor Name: Judge Dr Martin Borowsky
Synopsis: On October 17, 2024, the Erfurt Regional Court in Germany took into account the inherent rights of nature when calculating damages from diesel emissions.

Title: Panama National Aeronaval Service Resolution: creating an environmental committee
Theoretical Framing: x
Initiating Actor Name: National Aeronaval Service (SENAN); Callie Veelenturf
Synopsis: In June 2022, Panama’s National Aeronaval Service (SENAN) passed a resolution creating an Environmental Committee charged with defending the Rights of Nature.

Title: Sault Tribe (USA) Resolution: Rights of Nature
Theoretical Framing: "NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED, that Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa Indians now establishes the Rights of More than Human Relatives and operate in a manner not to threaten and endanger the clean air, freshwater and land resources and necessary habitats for More than Human Relatives, and take the steps to conduct business in a way necessary to protect More than Human Relatives including winged, swimmers, insects, plants, 4-legged, fresh water, air, mineral and land as part of our treaty foods and foundational relationships for future generations of Anishinaabeg"
Initiating Actor Name: Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa Indians
Synopsis: The Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa Indians in northern Michigan passed a resolution in defense of the tribe’s first family: its natural resources.

Title: Falmouth (USA) Resolution: rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "the call for stronger environmental protection is world-wide; and one major response has been the mass adoption of legal enactments recognizing that humanity is part of Nature, is responsible for Nature's care, and should recognize Nature -- the entire, inter-dependent community of life on Earth -- as having the right to exist, persist, regenerate, and be restored."
Initiating Actor Name: Dr. Elizabeth Gladfelter and local residents
Synopsis: On April 22, 2024, the Town of Falmouth in Cape Cod, Massachusetts, adopted a resolution recognizing the rights of nature.

Title: Ecuador Constitutional Court Case: rights of marine ecosystems
Theoretical Framing: "Therefore, the purpose of this limitation is to...protect other elements of marine-coastal ecosystems, including non-tradable biotic factors, such as whales, sea turtles, sharks, manta rays, corals, among others as well as duty constitutional right to live in harmony with nature that allows limiting anthropogenic activities in order to ensure the balance of trophic chains."
Initiating Actor Name: Constitutional Court of Ecuador
Synopsis: In late-2024, the Constitutional Court of Ecuador ruled that coastal marine ecosystems have legal rights that must be protected.

Title: Spain Constitutional Court Case: Mar Menor Act of 2022
Theoretical Framing: constitutional
Initiating Actor Name: Constitutional Court of Spain
Synopsis: On November 20, 2024, Spain's constitutional court ruled that the Mar Menor Act of 2022 establishing legal rights of the Mar Menor Lagoon is constitutional.

Title: Panama Supreme Court Case: Sierra Llorona Nature Reserve
Theoretical Framing: “duty to ensure the application of all administrative, legal and/or technical measures…necessary to prevent and restrict the effects of human activities that may contribute to the extinction of species, the destruction of ecosystems or the permanent alteration of natural cycles and the climate.."

Synopsis: In June 2024, Panama’s Supreme Court suspended the Caribbean Corridor road project through the Sierra Llorona Nature Reserve, citing rights of nature violations

Title: Panama Ministerial Decree: Saboga Wildlife Refuge
Theoretical Framing: "The objectives of the creation of the new National Wildlife Refuge outlined by the Ministry of the Environment include promoting the conservation of marine turtles, maintaining ecological cycles, restoring coral reefs, developing ecotourism initiatives, impulsing conservation efforts, and “recognizing and defending the rights of nature, as a subject of rights, in the Pearl Islands Archipelago" "Article 3. ESTABLISH as objectives of the Wildlife Refuge Saboga, the following: Recognize and defend the rights of nature, as a subject of rights, in the Pearl Archipelago”
Initiating Actor Name: Ministry of the Environment of Panama; The Leatherback Project
Synopsis: On September 22, 2024, Panama's Ministry of Environment passed a resolution creating The Saboga Wildlife Refuge, including habitats for endangered sea turtles.

Title: UN General Assembly Resolution on Sustainable Development: Harmony with Nature
Theoretical Framing: "Recognizing also that some countries recognize the rights of nature or Mother Earth in the context of the promotion of sustainable development, and expressing the conviction that, in order to achieve a just balance among the economic, social and environmental needs of present and future generations, it is necessary to promote harmony with nature..."
Initiating Actor Name: UN
Synopsis: On December 19th, 2024, the UN General Assembly adopted a Resolution on Sustainable Development: Harmony with Nature.

Title: Marinduque Province (Philippines) Environmental Code: recognizing rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "all matters relating to the right to a healthy environment of the people and their right to development which should be consistent with the recognition and protection of the rights of nature.”
Initiating Actor Name: Rights of Nature PH, Marinduque Provincial Government
Synopsis: In 2025, the island of Marinduque, Philippines, made significant strides in environmental protection by enacting the Marinduque Environment Code.

Title: Philippines Ecosystem and Natural Capital Accounting System Act
Theoretical Framing: "Recognize natural ecosystems as an integral part of our patrimony and heritage...Protect and promote ecological balance and resilience, and advance the right of the people to live in harmony with nature..."
Initiating Actor Name: Government of the Philippines

Title: Infanta (Philippines) Municipal Ordinance: rights of the Agos River and Sierra Madre

Title: Paris Grants Honorary Citizenship to the Seine River
Theoretical Framing: "By attributing intentions to the river, by presenting it as an entity to be protected from a supposedly destructive humanity, you are choosing a dualistic and simplistic vision in which we oppose man to nature. However, it is man's responsibility to protect and defend nature. Our humanist convictions refuse to let him lose this essential role."

Synopsis: In February 2025, Paris granted honorary citizenship to the Seine River and emphasized the necessity to grant real legal status to the river.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal, 6th International Hearing: New York 2024/2025

(paragraph 2)

Synopsis: In October, Motion 056 “Advancing an Ethical Human–Ocean Relationship” was adopted by the IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025 at it's session in Abu Dhabi.

Title: Utrecht (Netherlands) Municipal Motion: rights for nature

In this way, Utrecht gives a voice to those and that which cannot stand up for themselves; animals, nature and the environment.”

Title: Southern Chiefs’ Organization of Manitoba (Canada) court case: legal personhood for Lake Winnipeg

Now she is dying as Manitoba Hydro profits,” said SCO Grand Chief Jerry Daniels. “It is time for us all to stand up for Lake Winnipeg and say no more. We must change our ways to save her life.”

Initiating Actor Name: The id·eau association

Title: Rhode Island (USA) Resolution: rights of natural ecosystems

Title: Peru Regional Ordinance: Lake Titicaca as a subject of law of regional interest

Title: Minas Gerais State (Brazil) Law: rights of the Doce River

Initiating Actor Name: Judith Krom (Party for the Animals)

Title: Colombia National Law: Rights of the Putumayo River

Synopsis: In 2024, the Yurok Tribe approved a Tribal Ordinance establishing the Rights of the Heyhl-keek ‘We-roy (Klamath River), following an earlier 2019 Resolution.

Theoretical Framing:
“Por medio de la cual se dictan disposiciones con objeto de reconocer a los ríos y otras fuentes hídricas como sujetos de derechos, y crear el sistema nacional de protección y conservación integral de ríos con especiales condiciones hidrogeológicas, paisajísticas, históricas, ecológicas y culturales.” (“Proyecto de ley” webpage)

“Through which provisions are issued to recognize rivers and other water sources as subjects of rights, and to create the national system for the comprehensive protection and conservation of rivers with special hydrogeological, landscape, historical, ecological, and cultural conditions.”

This should also be achieved by designating the Lake system as a Wetland of International Importance under the Ramsar Convention.” (Page 2)

Synopsis: In 2018, a civil society declaration was signed, calling for actions to protect the Lake Ohrid ecosystem, including pollution control and Ramsar status.

Title: Colombia Law: rights of Caquetá River
Theoretical Framing:
“La presente iniciativa legislativa tiene por objeto garantizar la protección, conservación, mantenimiento y restauración del Río Caquetá, su cuenca y afluentes, mediante su reconocimiento como entidad sujeto de derechos, para hacerle frente a las problemáticas ambientales del Río que se han derivado principalmente por causa de la minería ilegal que usa mercurio para la recuperación de oro del lecho del río, playas, playones y áreas de inundación. Recordando que este Río es eje axial de la Amazonía colombiana, pulmón del mundo, su reconocimiento como sujeto de derechos y las medidas para su conservación impacta de manera positiva en el bienestar general de todos los colombianos.” (Page 2)

“The purpose of this legislative initiative is to guarantee the protection, conservation, maintenance, and restoration of the Caquetá River, its basin, and tributaries by recognizing it as an entity with rights, in order to address the environmental problems of the river that have arisen mainly due to illegal mining that uses mercury to recover gold from the riverbed, beaches, sandbanks, and flood areas. Recalling that this river is the backbone of the Colombian Amazon, the lungs of the world, its recognition as a subject of rights and the measures for its conservation have a positive impact on the general well-being of all Colombians.” (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: Senador Miguel Ángel Barreto Castillo, Senador Inti Raúl Asprilla Reyes, Senador Didier Lobo Chinchilla
Synopsis: In 2024, Colombia advanced a bill to grant rights to the Caquetá River and appoint a commission to represent its legal interests.

Title: Colombian Law: rights of the Patía River Basin
Theoretical Framing:
“Por medio del cual se reconoce la Cuenca del Río Patía, como sujeto de derechos, se establecen medidas para detener su destrucción, protegerla, recuperarla, restaurarla y conservarla y se dictan otras disposiciones.” (Page 1)

“Whereby the Patía River Basin is recognized as a subject of rights, measures are established to halt its destruction, protect it, recover it, restore it, and conserve it, and other provisions are enacted.” (Page 1)

Synopsis: In 2025, Colombia introduced a bill to grant rights to the Patía River, ban mining in its upper basin, and create a commission to protect the river.

Title: Ecuador Management Plan for Protected Areas of Galapagos Good Living
Theoretical Framing: “Every person, community, people, or nationality may demand that public authorities comply with the rights of nature. To apply and interpret these rights, the principles established in the Constitution will be observed, as appropriate.”

Synopsis: In 2014, the Management Plan for Protected Areas of Galapagos Good Living included the respect for Rights of Nature as a guiding principle for management.

Title: Durango (USA) Resolution: rights for the Animas River
Theoretical Framing: "We ask the city of Durango to recognize the inherent rights of the Animas River as it flows through Durango city limits, acknowledging the social, cultural, and spiritual values of the river in addition to its economic value. We recommend that the City of Durango consider the natural ecological systems of the Animas River in all local decision-making." (Page 78)
Initiating Actor Name: Students of Fort Lewis College
Synopsis: In November 2025, a student-led resolution acknowledging inherent rights for the Animas River was adopted by the Durango City Council.

Title: International Rights of Nature Tribunal Declaration: A New Pledge for Mother Nature 2025
Theoretical Framing: "Considering the reality of the dominant society of the Amazon that views the reserve of its natural resources to be exploited continues to be the main cause of its destruction. On the contrary, we believe that the Amazon, with its ecosystems, animal,plant species, and rich biodiversity, natural medicines and its vital and reproductive cycles, should be considered as a subject of rights together with the Indigenous Peoples, and other communities that inhabit it." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: International Rights of Nature Tribunal
Synopsis: In November 2025, the 6th International Rights of Nature Tribunal was held at COP30 in Belém, Brazil, where “A New Pledge for Mother Nature” was launched.

Title: Satipo (Perú) Municipal Ordinance: Rights of Stingless Bees
Theoretical Framing: "The proposal establishes that, in order to formally declare Amazonian stingless bees and their habitat as subjects of rights, recognizing their intrinsic rights, such as to exist, maintain healthy populations, live in a healthy environment, conserve and regenerate their habitat, and linking their protection to the comprehensive conservation of the Amazon, it finally establishes that within the province of Satipo, the use of existing pesticides, including cosmetic pesticides and agricultural chemicals, that are harmful or pose an unacceptable risk to stingless bees is prohibited; It also prohibits the burning of hives, honeycombs, or swarms in protected areas of the province." (Page 1)

Synopsis: On October 21, 2025, the provincial municipality of Satipo, Peru approved Ordinance N° 33, declaring the rights of stingless bees.

Title: Eastern Band of Cherokee Tribal Resolution: Rights of the Longperson Water System
Theoretical Framing: "NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians in Council assembled, at which a quorum is present, that the Tribal Council hereby recognizes the inherent rights of the Longperson, a natural entity within the boundary and Ancestral homelands of the Cherokee people"
Initiating Actor Name: National American Indian Women’s Association Daughters
Synopsis: On January 8, 2026, the Eastern Band of Cherokee passed the Longperson Rights of Nature Resolution, recognizing it as living relative with inherent rights.

Title: Iceland Constitutional Referendum of 2011
Theoretical Framing: “Iceland’s nature is the foundation of life in the country. Everyone is under obligation to respect it and protect it. Everyone shall by law be ensured the right to a healthy environment, fresh water, clean air and unspoiled nature. This means maintenance of life and land and protection of sites of natural interest, unpopulated wilderness, vegetation and soil. Previous damage shall be repaired to the extent possible. The use of natural resources shall be managed so as to minimise their depletion in the long term with respect for the rights of nature and future generations.” (Article 33. Iceland’s nature and environment)
Initiating Actor Name: Constitutional Council (Stjórnlagaráð)
Synopsis: In 2011, the Constitutional Council submitted a draft to the Icelandic Parliament that included the rights of nature in an amended national constitution.

Title: Nauta (Peru) Municipal Ordinance: rights of stingless bees
Theoretical Framing: " formally declare Amazonian stingless bees and their habitat as subjects of rights, recognizing their intrinsic rights, such as to exist, maintain healthy populations, live in a healthy environment, conserve and regenerate their habitat, and linking their protection to the comprehensive conservation of the Amazon"
Initiating Actor Name: Dr. Rosa Vásquez Espinoza (Amazon Research Internacional); Earth Law Center; Loreto Meliponiculturists Association; Kukama-Kukamiria Indigenous Communities
Synopsis: On December 22, 2025, the Provincial Municipality of Nauta-Loreto approved a local ordinance recognizing the rights of native stingless bees.

Title: Alderville First Nation Resolution: Rights of Rice Lake
Theoretical Framing: "This resolution is part of the mino bimaadiziwin, which is our way of living life, in a positive way, and other nations around the world have similar concepts.” (Chief Simpson). The concept is similar to Buen Vivir / sumak kawsay (Quechua) which emphasize a life in harmony with nature and the community, seeking balance and fulfillment for all its members. A guardian council will be established to be the representative and voice of the lake, comprised of “townships, municipalities, conservation groups, anglers, hunters, harvesters – anyone that has a say in Rice Lake will have a seat at that guardians council."

Title: Provence (France) Citizen Declaration: rights of the Durance River
Theoretical Framing: "As a subject of law, the Durance River possesses the following fundamental rights: – the right to exist, to flourish, and to flow freely; – the right to respect for its natural cycles; – the right to fulfill its essential ecological functions; – the right not to be polluted; – the right to sustainably supply and be supplied by aquifers; – the right to the maintenance of its indigenous biodiversity; – the right to natural regeneration and restoration; – the right to take legal action."
Initiating Actor Name: SOS Durance Vivante; Notre Affaire à Tous
Synopsis: In November 2023, the Declaration of the Rights of Durance was drafted in order to support the recognition of the river as a legal natural entity.

Title: Provence (France) Citizen Declaration: Rights of the Arc River
Theoretical Framing: "As a legal person, the Arc and its watershed possess the following rights: the right to exist, to live and to flow freely in accordance with its natural cycles; the right to fulfill its essential ecological functions; the right to conservation, regeneration and restoration; the right to be protected from all pollution; the right to maintain and protect its flood expansion zones; the right to supply and be supplied by all its tributaries and aquifers; the right to take legal action through its representatives"
Initiating Actor Name: Arc Fleuve Vivant Association
Synopsis: In November 2023, the Declaration of the Rights of the Arc River was drafted in order to support its recognition as a legal natural entity.

Title: Argentina court case: rights of Lola the puma
Theoretical Framing: The cougar Lola Limon was recognized as a "subject of rights." Lola was granted “complete freedom, free of any measure or legal restriction, with definitive legal custody granted to the Interactive Ecopark of the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires.”

Title: Ecuador A'i Cofán of Sinangoe Indigenous Law

Theoretical Framing: The "territory is alive, it is sacred; the good spirits that help us heal live here, and the Shamans contact them to attract the animals that help the family live and eat healthy food; it is the only space we have left to continue developing and growing, to continue living and connecting with nature." The Law aims to "guarantee the healthy and harmonious survival of the A'i People, and our close and significant relationship with our territory, considered a living being, to foster a sustainable and balanced environment."

Title: Global Citizens' Assembly call for ecocide
Theoretical Framing: "Ecocide has to be codified as a crime in the international and national laws, applicable to governments and corporations."

Title: Sweden Declaration on the Rights of Lake Vättern
Theoretical Framing: "Through this declaration, we recognize Lake Vättern's irrevocable right to exist, flourish and develop naturally, in accordance with the Universal Declaration of the Rights of Mother Earth."

Title: Loyalty Islands Province Environmental Code 2023: rights of sharks and sea turtles

Title: Citizen Declaration of the Rights and Freedom of Dolphins and Whales
Theoretical Framing:
1. "All dolphins and whales are individuals possessing self-awareness, intellect and feelings." 2. "All dolphins and whales are born and must remain free, they have the right to live and move freely in their natural environment, from which they may not be forcibly removed, and they must not be separated from the family groups to which they belong." 
6. "Dolphins and whales have the right to have their culture protected from being interfered with. Observation of dolphins and whales can only take place in natural conditions and should be regulated by international norms that guarantee their peace and security." 10. "The rights and freedoms established by this Declaration should be protected by international and national legislation. (e) The right to a healthy natural environment and natural biodiversity. (f) The right to natural water flow. (g) The right to perform its natural ecosystem functions. (h) The right to be free of activities that infringe upon the rights established under pars. (a) to (g)." (Page 2)

Title: Test Valley (UK) District Council Motion: Rights of the Test River
Theoretical Framing: “The right to flow – unimpeded, seasonal, and natural; the right to perform essential functions within the ecosystem; the right to be free from pollution and contamination.” (Page 1-2)

Title: Alameda (USA) Resolution: Safe Passage of Whales
Theoretical Framing: "RESOLVED, That the City of Alameda supports the free and safe passage of all whales and sea turtles that traverse our coastal waters and supports the state of California’s commitment to preventing whale and sea turtle entanglements and vessel strikes, commitment to investing in whale-safe fisheries by supporting innovative technologies like on-demand fishing gear, and commitment to ensuring that current and future generations of Californians can enjoy our state’s rich marine biodiversity." (Page 2)

Title: Perú National Law: Stingless Bees a Matter of National Interest
Theoretical Framing: "Beekeeping and the agro-industrial activity of bee products are declared to be of national interest due to their economic, social, and ecological importance. The domestic bee (Apis mellifera) must be protected, native stingless bees or meliponas, and native bee species as beneficial insects, as well as bee and melipona flora as national treasures, preventing their indiscriminate felling and promoting their reforestation, conservation, scientific research, and promotion as a sustainable productive activity." The short term ambition is to serve as a foundation in supporting the inclusion of Ocean Rights in the 2023 United Nations Omnibus Resolution on ‘Oceans and Law of the Seas.’ This framework is founded on a profound respect for the Ocean’s inherent right to health and restoration, and acknowledges the Ocean’s fundamental role in sustaining all life on the planet." (Page 1)

Title: Moananui Sanctuary Agreement
Theoretical Framing: “Reaffirming our commitment to recognising the mana (spiritual authority, prestige, and power) and mauri (life force, vital essence), alongside the intrinsic value and inherent rights of whales and Moananui.” (Preamble)

Title: Belize Court Case: Rights of the Barrier Reef
Theoretical Framing:
"In her witness statement, Dr McField described coral reefs as 'actual living ecosystems made up of a vast assemblage of plants, animals and microbial life that function together to sustain the reef framework and to produce a variety of ecosystem services benefiting the people of Belize, the Wider Caribbean and the world.'" "Dr McField asserted that the 'living reef ecosystem and the services it provides are not the ‘property’ of anyone'." (Page 10)

Synopsis: In 2011, the Court of Appeal of Belize upheld a witness testimony that acknowledged coral reefs as living and complex ecosystems that are not property.

Title: Denmark Citizen Proposal: Green Constitution
Theoretical Framing:
"The citizens' assembly must include at least one fifth of representatives of stakeholders who are not currently living Danes (cf. the bio-general good, i.e. the future, people outside Denmark and nature)" (Kapitel VI). 

"In the administration of justice, Danish citizens, species, ecosystems, cycles and ecosystems and species native to Denmark, as well as species outside or across the borders of the kingdom, have the status of legal persons" (Kapitel VII).

"No one may make a living from activities that directly or indirectly harm the bio-general well-being in Denmark, in other countries, on planet Earth - now or in the future" (Kapitel IX).
Initiating Actor Name: Naturens Grundlovsmøde

Title: France Citizen Declaration: Rights of the Garonne River
Theoretical Framing: "Right to flow freely, right to health and preservation of its integrity, right to fulfill its essential functions in its ecosystem, right to regeneration and restoration, right to take legal action and be represented." (Page 4)

Synopsis: The Citizens' Assembly of Haute-Garonne declared itself in favor of granting legal status to the Garonne River.

Title: Switzerland Criminal Court Case: The Life of a Mouse and Two Foxes Crossing the Roadway
Theoretical Framing: To expect a driver to simply run over vertebrate animals when they appear on a road is irreconcilable with the innate human respect for all life belonging to the animal kingdom. The purpose of such respect is to preserve, rather than destroy, non-human animal life.
Initiating Actor Name: Female Citizen
Synopsis: Whether two foxes or a single mouse, the Swiss Federal Supreme Court ruled that innate human respect for all animal life means protecting, not destroying it.

Title: Switzerland: Los Cedros Freedom of Information Request on IACHR Advisory Opinion 32/25
Theoretical Framing: With a letter dated October 31, 2025, the Ecuadorian cloud forest Los Cedros and its inhabitants submitted a joint freedom of information request to the Federal Department of Foreign Affairs (FDFA) of Switzerland and the Inter-American Court of Human Rights (I/A Court H.R.) in Costa Rica. Acting as a journalist under Article 10, paragraph 4(a) FoIA and Article 10 ECHR, Los Cedros explained that it intends to publish its journalistic work on the FDFA’s cooperation with the Inter-American Court of Human Rights. From the proceeds of this work, the Ecuadorian cloud forest Los Cedros and its endangered wildlife, located within the Key Biodiversity Area (KBA) Toisán-Cuellaje-Los Cedros Cordillera, will benefit, providing crucial resources to protect this unique ecosystem of international importance from biodiversity loss.
Initiating Actor Name: Los Cedros ecosystem; Citizen (legal representative)
Synopsis: Journalistic Endeavours – Los Cedros and Its Cloud Forest Inhabitants Set Out to Cover Swiss Support for the Inter-American Court of Human Rights.

Title: Berkeley (USA) Resolution: Safe Passage of Whales, Sea Turtles, and Marine Animals
Theoretical Framing: "The City of Berkeley supports the free and safe passage of all whales and sea turtles that traverse our coastal waters; and supports the state of California’s commitment to preventing whale and sea turtle entanglements and vessel strikes; and commitment to ensuring that current and future generations of Californians can enjoy our state’s rich marine biodiversity." (Page 3)
Initiating Actor Name: Councilmember Cecilia Lunaparra (Author) and Councilmember Shoshana O’Keefe (Co-Sponsor)

Title: Green Bay (USA) Resolution: Rights of Nature
Theoretical Framing:
"Recommend the Sustainability Commission to draft a Rights of Nature resolution for the Common Council’s consideration. This resolution could be modeled on Milwaukee County’s 2023 resolution and tailored to Green Bay’s unique natural assets. Key elements for inclusion in the resolution text and for the drafting process:

1. Recognition of local ecosystems – Affirm that the Bay of Green Bay, the Fox and East Rivers, and associated wetlands possess the right to clean water, habitat integrity, and restoration.

2. Rights-based standards – Borrow wording from the Menominee River declaration—e.g., the right to “abundant, pure, clean, unpolluted water” and freedom from activities that infringe upon these rights.

3. Collaborative drafting – Engage Tribal Nations and community partners to ensure the resolution reflects Indigenous knowledge and local values."
Initiating Actor Name: Sustainability Commission of the City of Green Bay

Title: Gouda (Netherlands) Municipal Bylaw: nature-inclusive participation
Theoretical Framing: "This means that residents and nature representatives are involved in a timely manner, that the process is clear, that information is readily available, and that there is clear feedback on what has been done with the input."
Initiating Actor Name: Wouter Kraidy (council member, Party for the Animals)

Title: Finland Constitutional Citizens Initiative: fundamental rights of nature
Theoretical Framing: "The defense of the fundamental rights of natural parts is expanded from the requirement of a party to the dispute so that everyone can use the power to speak on behalf of nature.(1) The defense of nature is argued by examining through scientific knowledge what is good for nature and in accordance with the interests of natural parts. On the other hand, knowledge and interest do not necessarily have to be attached to the same subject, but sometimes external (for example, extensive systemic) information may be needed to determine the interest.(2) The “silence” of nature, to which the title of our citizens’ initiative refers, means a similar legal position to that of anyone who needs a representative or advocate in court. In the case of nature, it is a matter of silence in relation to human language and the logic and communication structure of the legal system built by humans. Nature and its parts must be taken into account in decision-making situations as independent legal subjects that may have interests and a different voice from human goals." (Page 2, Translated from Finnish)

Title: Mexico Law on Ecological Equilibrium and Environmental Protection

Theoretical Framing: "The Venice Lagoon is a unique ecosystem, shaped by the evolution of nature and the interventions of the humans who have inhabited it for millennia. Synopsis: On November 20 2025, a Declaration of the Rights of the Venice Lagoon was launched, recognizing the lagoon as a legal entity within a complex urban environment.

Theoretical Framing: "The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025, at its session in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates: 1.
