Theoretical Framing: "The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025, at its session in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates: 1. DECLARES that unlawful or wanton acts committed with knowledge that there is a substantial likelihood of severe and either widespread or long-term damage to the environment being caused by those acts, should be prosecuted as the crime of ecocide under both national and international law; ...7. RECOMMENDS that States Parties to the Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court evaluate whether “widespread, long-term and severe damage to the natural environment”, while encompassed within prohibitions against War Crimes under Article 8(2) (b)(iv), could be clarified by amending the Statute to make ecocide a crime explicitly within the jurisdiction of the Court, in peacetime as well as during armed conflict." (Pages 1-2)
Initiating Actor Name: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN), The Wildlife Trusts, Born Free Foundation, Gallifrey Foundation, Earth League International, Earth Law Center, Synchronicity Earth, A Rocha Ghana, Bangladesh Environmental Lawyers’ Association, The Conflict and Environment Observatory, Africa Institute for Energy Governance, Environment and Conservation Organisations of Aotearoa New Zealand, Friendship Bangladesh, Centro Mexicano de Derecho Ambiental (CEMDA), Fundación Oxígeno, la Fundación Española para la Defensa del Patrimonio Geológico y Minero, SEBICOP (Sociedad Española de Biología de Conservación de las Plantas), Vitalis, Fundación Charles Darwin and Fundación Futuro Latinoamericano Ecuador, Pronaturaleza Peru, LIDEMA Bolivia, Fundación Hábitat y Desarrollo Argentina, Preserve Planet Costa Rica, ARPEMG Brazil, COICA (Coordinadora de las Organizaciones Indígenas de la Cuenca Amazónica), AIDESEP Peru

Title: IUCN 2025 Motion 067: living in harmony with rivers through rights of nature & ecocentric law
Theoretical Framing: "The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025, at its session in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates: 1. RECOMMENDS that IUCN Members and Commissions promote the protection and restoration of rivers and wetlands through: a. supporting the work of the World Commission on Environmental Law (WCEL) on rights of nature, and related initiatives; b. promoting cross-border cooperation and facilitating collaboration and knowledge-sharing; c. harmonising the protection and restoration of rivers with other rights-based campaigns for nature; and d. ensuring that Indigenous peoples and Local communities, including those represented by IUCN Indigenous Peoples’ Organisations, as well as youth and women, play a central role;" (Pages 1-2)
Initiating Actor Name: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)

Title: IUCN 2025 Motion 070: integrating science, rights-based approaches, and ecological recovery
Theoretical Framing: "The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025, at its session in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates: 1. REQUESTS the Director General and IUCN Members to: a. ensure that deep incorporation and accountability of existing resolutions, using best available scientific evidence across Commissions, guides decision-making for rewilding, restoration and the conservation of species in human-wildlife conflict, prioritizing ecological integrity and wholistic governance; b. respect the customary practices of Indigenous peoples and Local communities that contribute to the sustainable use of, and ethical coexistence with wildlife;" (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)

Title: International Declaration of Reef Rights 2007
Theoretical Framing: "All coral reefs have the right to be free from: • Over-fishing and destructive fishing • Pollution • Human caused coral bleaching and ocean acidification • Direct damage from divers, snorkelers and boaters • Damage due to poorly planned coastal development • Coral diseases"
Initiating Actor Name: Reef Check

Title: Rother (UK) District Council Motion: rights of trees
Theoretical Framing: "This Council supports the introduction into law of legislation which would require those taking decisions affecting trees to have regard to the trees' rights" "This Council therefore: 1. welcomes the decision of Crowhurst Parish Council to support “personhood status” for the 1066 Yew tree." (Page 1)
Initiating Actor Name: Councillors Nicola McLaren and Sue Burton

Title: Colombia National Law: rights of the Aburrá River
Theoretical Framing:
“Article 1. Purpose. The Aburrá River, its basin and tributaries are hereby declared subjects of rights for their conservation, protection, maintenance and restoration.”

"Article 2. Collegiate Body and Protection of the Aburrá River (COPRA).
The Committee is hereby created as the legal representative of the Aburrá River, led by the Regional Autonomous Corporations and the Metropolitan Area of the Aburrá Valley. It will include the participation of public entities, social and environmental organizations, and risk management entities related to the river basin, in order to coordinate the
various actions and their budgetary integration."
Initiating Actor Name: Paola Holguín, Juan Espinal
Synopsis: In August 2025, the Aburrá River (also called the Medellín River) was declared a subject of rights through Law 2533 of Colombia.

Title: Bucaramanga (Colombia) Court Case: rights of the Santurbán Páramo
Theoretical Framing:
"SECOND. TO RECOGNIZE the Santurbán Páramo as an entity subject to the rights of protection, conservation, maintenance, and restoration at the expense of the State and the communities that inhabit it and depend on its environmental services, in accordance with the provisions stated in the main part of this provision. For this purpose, the creation of this state implies, among other things, the following legal and practical consequences:
- Priority Protection: Administrative and judicial decisions must consider the preservation of the integrity and health of the Santurbán Páramo as an end in itself, not solely as a means for human well-being. - Legal Representation: The designation of a legal representative (custodian or guardian) of the Páramo, which could be a public entity, a civil organization, or a consortium of local communities, would be authorized, with the power to initiate judicial and administrative actions on behalf of the ecosystem. - Proactive Precautionary Measures: The adoption of preventive precautionary measures would be facilitated against any threat that jeopardizes its integrity, rigorously applying the principle of precaution. - Harmonization of Policies: All sectoral policies (mining, energy, agriculture, etc.) should be harmonized with the preservation of the Santurbán Páramo, integrating its conservation and sustainable use in development plans."
Initiating Actor Name: Municipality of Bucaramanga; Administrative Court of Santander
Synopsis: On July 21, 2025, the Administrative Court of Santander in Colombia issued a ruling that recognized the Santurbán Páramo as a subject of rights.

Title: France Citizen Declaration: Rights of the Seine River
Theoretical Framing: "the right to exist, to thrive and to evolve, the right to spring forth and flow freely, and the right not to be polluted."
Initiating Actor Name: Wild Legal

Title: East Hampshire (UK) District Council Motion: rights of rivers and chalkstreams
Theoretical Framing: "This Council resolves to: 1. Recognise the River Wey, Meon and Rother and all chalk streams and our rivers in the district as living systems with an intrinsic 'right' to exist, thrive, and evolve — aligning with emerging global principles of the rights of nature, as outlined in the Universal Declaration of rivers rights"
Initiating Actor Name: Councillor Ginny Boxall

Title: Rother (UK) District Council Motion: rights of rivers
Theoretical Framing: “This Council supports the growing global movement for rights for nature in general and for rivers in particular, as a framework for rethinking the relationship between human beings and the natural world. This Council supports the introduction into law of the following rights for rivers: the right to flow; the right to perform essential functions within their ecosystems; the right to be free from pollution; the right to feed, and to be fed by, sustainable aquifers; the right to native biodiversity; the right to regeneration and restoration; and the right to have an articulated voice in the community."
Initiating Actor Name: Councillors Sue Burton and Nicola McLaren
Synopsis: On September 15, 2025, the Rother District Council in Sussex, England passed the Rights for Rothers Rivers Motion.

Title: Wealden (UK) District Council Motion: rights of rivers
Theoretical Framing: "supports the growing global movement for rights for nature in general and for rivers in particular as a framework for rethinking the relationship between human beings and the rest of the world."
Initiating Actor Name: Councilors Rachel Millward and Paul Coleshill

Title: Turkey Ecocide Law Citizens Initiative
Theoretical Framing: "The Crime of Ecocide ARTICLE 77/A: (1) A person who endangers the life of humans or other living beings in the natural or cultural environment, commits an act that may cause serious damage to natural or cultural assets, or commits another unlawful act that creates a risk of causing irreparable damage to the entire ecosystem in the short term, shall be sentenced to life imprisonment, and shall also be sentenced to a fine equal to ten times the material benefits obtained from the commission of the crime and the economic gain resulting from their valuation or transformation. If the crime of ecocide results in damage to an entire ecosystem that cannot be remedied in the short term, the perpetrator shall be sentenced to aggravated life imprisonment; if the crime is committed negligently, the perpetrator shall be sentenced to twenty years imprisonment, and a judicial fine of twenty times the amount of the material benefits obtained from the crime and the economic gain resulting from their valuation or transformation shall also be imposed."
Initiating Actor Name: Citizens; MP Nimet Özdemir
Synopsis: In March 2025, a bill to amend the Turkish Penal Code No. 5237 of 2004 to include a crime of ecocide, was submitted to the national assembly.

Title: Switzerland: Mar Menor FOI Request on Climate and Biodiversity to the Federal Office of Justice
Theoretical Framing: The Swiss Federal Office of Justice responded that under Swiss FoIA, any person – in the FOJ’s view, the term ‘person’ refers to natural persons – may request access to official documents. Since no special eligibility is required, the FOJ did not consider it necessary to determine whether Mar Menor has legal personality. On 14 November 2025, the FDPIC formally stated it could not enter into mediation because the FOJ had not determined whether Mar Menor was regarded as the person submitting the request and had left open the question of whether Mar Menor qualifies as a “person” within the meaning of Article 6 (1) FoIA. The FDPIC criticized the FOJ’s handling of the information request, noting that the question of whether an access request is submitted by a natural or legal person must be clarified by the competent authority during the access request procedure, as this is a prerequisite for the right of access under Article 6 FoIA.
Initiating Actor Name: Mar Menor ecosystem; Spanish association (legal representative)
Synopsis: Mar Menor sought environmental information from the Federal Office of Justice of Switzerland, but faced a denial of justice.

Title: Austria Citizens Initiative Petition: constitutional amendment for nature as a legal person
Theoretical Framing: "Past experience clearly shows that protecting nature purely out of human interest has been insufficient for its preservation. Therefore, nature, or parts of it, must no longer be protected for our well-being, but for its own sake. Granting nature legal capacity and the capacity to be a party to legal proceedings is thus a logical consequence"
Initiating Actor Name: MP Julia Elisabeth Herr; citizens
Synopsis: In November 2025 a petition concerning "Nature's capacity for independent rights – suggestion to amend the Federal Constitution and federal laws" was submitted.

Title: Germany Administrative Submission: Hambach Lignite Mine
Theoretical Framing: "The aim of this submission is not confrontation, but rather the activation of the constitutional duty to protect the natural foundations of life."
Initiating Actor Name: Hans Leo Bader, Helmut Scheel, Sabina Rothe
Synopsis: On 16 October 2025, a Citizen Submission regarding the Hambach Lignite Mine was submitted to the German Ministry for the Environment

Title: Tyrol (Austria) Citizen Declaration: Together for a Living Isel

Synopsis: On 13 September 2025, residents of East Tyrol, Austria together with the Tyrolean Environmental Ombudsman, presented a declaration for a living Isel River.

Title: Berlin (Germany) Draft Law: rights of the Spree River
Theoretical Framing: "This law recognizes the legal capacity of the Spree River. The Spree is an ecological person and is to be treated as a legal entity. It is a legal subject that can invoke its rights, defend them in court and in other ways, and advocate for its rights."
Initiating Actor Name: Rechte der Natur e.V.; Symbiotic Lab; Green Legal Impact
Synopsis: In October 2025, a group of citizens launched a draft law to recognize the rights of the Spree River in Berlin as a legal entity.

Title: Costa Rica Biodiversity Law of 1998
Theoretical Framing: "Respect for life in all its forms. All living beings have the right to life, regardless of their current or potential economic value." (Page 5, Translated from Spanish)

Title: Argentina Ecocide Bill: "Law on Special Forms of Environmental Crimes"
Theoretical Framing: "The purpose of this law is to classify ecocide and special forms of environmental crime for their systematic incorporation into the National Criminal Code." (Page 1, Translated from Spanish)
Initiating Actor Name: Senator Edith Terenzi

Title: Aotearoa (New Zealand) Tohorā Oranga Bill: legal personhood of whales
Theoretical Framing: "Indigenous Peoples of Te Moana-Nui-a-Kiwa (the Pacific Ocean) have always lived in harmony with the moana (ocean) and its inhabitants. Across the Pacific, many peoples see whales as a sentient beings and ancestors possessing inherent rights. With this unique connection comes generations of indigenous knowledge and histories with whales that informs the identities of many peoples across the Pacific"
Initiating Actor Name: Green Party MP Teanau Tuino
Synopsis: On 5 February 2026, MP Teanau Tuiono lodged a member's bill, the Tohorā Oranga Bill, in the New Zealand parliament, seeking to give whales inherent rights.

Title: France Ecocide Law
Theoretical Framing: "« Art. L. 231-3. – The offense referred to in Article L. 231-1 constitutes ecocide when committed intentionally. « The offenses provided for in Article L. 231-2, committed intentionally, also constitute ecocide when they result in serious and lasting damage to health, flora, fauna, or the quality of the air, soil, or water." (Page 16, Translated from French)

Title: FutureEverything (UK) Nature Governance: nature on the board
Theoretical Framing: "Nature will be a formal Board director of FutureEverything by September 2025 as part of our 30th anniversary. FutureEverything’s constitution will be amended to include Nature as a company director, affording Nature a say in, and voting rights on, all company matters – from business development, to organisational operations and the artistic programme." (Page 2)
Initiating Actor Name: FutureEverything
Synopsis: In October 2025, FutureEverything, a UK-based arts and technology organization, formally amended its constitution to include Nature as a board director.

Title: Switzerland Parliamentary Postulate: granting glaciers legal personality
Theoretical Framing: On 15 December 2017, Swiss National Council member Lisa Mazzone, together with 10 co-signatories, filed postulate 17.4312 to safeguard Switzerland’s iconic glaciers. With their rights-of-nature initiative, “Granting glaciers legal personhood and ensuring access to justice – a good opportunity for our country?”, they challenged the status of Swiss glaciers as mere objects of law and called for political measures to secure access to justice for these persistent bodies of dense ice, which form an integral part of Swiss identity.
Initiating Actor Name: Lisa Mazzone and Delphine Klopfenstein Broggini
Synopsis: In 2017, Swiss parliamentarians sought rights for glaciers, but the Federal Council rejected the proposal despite scientific warnings of their disappearance.

Title: Eastern Tropical Pacific Marine Corridor (CMAR) Management Proposal: Integration of Rights of Nature
Location: Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, and Panamá

Title: National Infrastructure Commission for Wales Recommendation: nature guardian

Title: Herefordshire (UK) Nature Governance: voice of the River Wye

"The role as a member of the Board holds voting rights. As a member of the NMB the role will be bound by the Nolan principles of selflessness, integrity, objectivity, accountability, openness, honesty and leadership."

Title: Scotland Citizens Petition: rights of River Clyde
Theoretical Framing:
"The petitioner is calling on the Scottish Parliament to urge the Scottish Government to grant the River Clyde, and potentially other rivers in Scotland, the legal right to personhood by:

• adopting the Universal Declaration on the Rights of Rivers
• appointing a Nature Director to act as a guardian of the River Clyde, with the responsibility for upholding its river rights
• considering whether an alternative mechanism should be established to act for the rights of the river, its inhabitants (human and non-human), and society at large."

Title: UK Citizens Petition: rights of rivers and rias
